name: John McGuire
subject: This isn’t for donors. It’s for warriors.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by John McGuire for Virginia
body:
Peter, I didn't learn loyalty in a classroom...
I learned it when the only thing between me and death was the man next to me, bleeding, praying, holding the line.[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/FtkiyCOOI_1kvIaMxfx2g27y5lDCdVO-oVv6vmz8jYS7oNJGqZFn2_rHR8ei_48zEA5oXGu6OWXkduHxlyieBuXQ-8jOS1MYuFtiKZ5SYBDHHEXK9R69lpJVmAol4qGiIzEapNPQ-AuVzgkxAnzOO4-v4nckR3UNjBOeP-u5Ki72L8L5nak1eDUsRIPK1rxcYdBR4rhpFoccVbI5LU0IETsvbpkWWssQquZb4oZh37m8L4jagL5m-oWiKMzvAf-kw-2ffNI7E-S8tLy7i6wE9AmpoDSl2c2zOGcFiiw2q3NQAARG26n2EDxYQyazFZrYJROAWquWgNjiZzrXBzmHVEogBvYRGakfgpiiYWwE68OnfSswrQReHGXkm8eDhlAYSTWSJJgV32HlsqD9VW0lY6-6klSeEFQisM0JZjifqXsF_D6TDnMQIQSm48-W296Xxf8TAWlHR-p_rQEJ5Z05OaiJa-tpaYi8XIC63hjFwHZD3Aju6Gvr5RcT-9mDZOX3z4hYmbxtrEhfxNExtQdRJbFAICV1KCBiTOd0bvOEnEH7pR6Nry8MAPW-63mlUd1K9UeGIsD6G1LpPOiD0FzmPFLHy_-5bXpSz_uP5yRnuKHrUsh7IRnF_8pjk9nMRThHUThgzJcHzcxayKV5z4Uf3C3KABXqtozmrh8BI-6wgRPyDpdxAKYmY4rCcKssvGCcR_sbsf8bKh8rKxQEjlEQpRcZn2ezuCdPEzUWYt_RxTsODflP2DscJH8d2HK3U5GU1gcEqc0-Q5MmQdBamkfeQ0lLVOzSsX63AAqbdI4qufo8m_RxA5YXC4U3E-hMejCX1B82eAKRGeGorblgwuS53BG-4Yn4mf2wMsHgolnbpy_TXIIcU_oaWxMJZL6ArZs7ovle1GcSHUUXZEU/orF_AhQxdgIYBVJ9hyMHAxsqxvYtbz3I/16)I was a Navy SEAL sniper. I've seen men carry each other with bones broken and breath shallow, because the mission didn't care how tired you were.
That's where I learned what brotherhood means.
And let me tell you, Peter, DC doesn't run on that kind of loyalty. Up here, it's traded for likes and lobbyist dinners. The cowards trying to flip this seat in '26 have never bled for anything in their lives.
But I have. And I'm still standing.
That's why I'm building something no pollster told me to create: A GOP Veteran Defender Membership Circle.
This isn't for donors. This is for fighters.
Just 20 names. Each one represents someone I can count on when the fire comes. Patriots who still believe in faith, grit, and country, and refuse to let the Left erase our flag or take down Trump.
Right now, I have 14 names.I'm holding the last 6 spots for those who've earned them.
And, Peter, I had a chance to look at your record and you DESERVE IT.
[So, If you’re still in this fight... if $5 or $10 won’t break you... then I’m asking: Peter join us, and claim your Defender Membership before 11:59 PM:](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/kZH_5qbyxNKrBI8q8_lFAJlG3m8DXrrkNOIXWO73ITBILmqtHexyXLLPnyjysd2tbtYnfr6y6sYAQWCOEiMsYIop7jmrTcZjohJMXHZVfvSAoVQBsGafwub8CA5t2Z9ScbPLwNtOHRRrDWdfReQkRMLbQZLXgbweiT3BlWsavXxN9fbcnQNS0PhftWZdpk9gvt2oFktTbaKTmp6iKtkXRs8AHHXttt_v4AMxxXGCjDIlH1qkkEYHBn1yVECi45JryL_MTycq5ZfoCApOBL19FZWYEDBjliDSonorB69C_1CWcdNqsIow1ng7c4ei2-whphAFjRlwduteFEoxZXBRvCw26Gv-DpBlV6CQM6-yR5Qy6X2-0aqTXfhZgygX9Q5AEOY5jQJPr71deq1uPrAyqYc-ldig9Z6pQjy8SD22bvRjy1p42h4j7BSO0tCAPuSxbnfU5daKwVGSFgZi4zKAe-0X3TDm7h7kSJTwiyYUyZHQvAqKj_15yK50dL_fnt9y6m6n0JyXTu8Zqb1iq0GdUrhUy4TMIiS83lMpEhf11Du8h8nDuRNIFbY4V3xtElg27Z_6onb-_fCqCK_nfiouhzR0E6dFwVMqJoyuS1fezRAqjDzhMS5SytzyKwR6fzblvpLdjUuhknfsc-7Ytn8gOUhb-2v0QiFsSoYsoivICUtUrHci91bxr9xQW-AgEwm_mxzJ01IZH5oaU4I2JEZrEx65__1JjzHu9hzGTUqR540baG9L9lUncRXQzB0ny-76mbOXcyUHEr0Nc7VV9ovT39ZrDJx0KMof0niZommaENA6SkvE8IPYSB3WYhM7LB19W-3dtZ2aWqG-qG9KMeQFzNFtVRKAc3ioNiYyxXFY_xCQimszSPEspac1kBvGGv8ay-1ALy251q7UoOY/vxTcMAm6RbiNdj8KadHS7XwD7n4581Nj/16)[DON’T WAIT: Peter

**Team, 1 in 3 Americans rely on mail-in voting to cast their ballot.**

If Trump succeeds in ending mail-in voting, seniors, rural voters, people with disabilities, military families, and so many others would be hit first and hardest.

This isn’t about “election integrity,” it’s about control.

Trump knows his agenda is unpopular, so he’s trying to shrink the electorate instead of winning it by gerrymandering districts and trying to end mail-in voting.

**It’s straight out of the authoritarian playbook.**

New Dems are fighting back in Congress, but we can’t do it alone. To stop this, we need to show overwhelming, undeniable public opposition to these attacks on our democracy.

**[That’s why we’re urgently collecting signatures from grassroots Democrats across the country. If you haven’t yet, will you add your name right now to our Defend Mail-In Voting petition? »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123093363/594335228/-1952426633?refcode=NDCAF_20260110_EMPET_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNDZlYzM3Ny04MGVlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wcFqqM8vTwqnbfovdO29TY86oHlpyuy4ChUqTUOXlG8=&emci=a1f049e3-9bed-f011-8194-000d3a11f903&emdi=d46ec377-80ee-f011-8194-000d3a11f903&ceid=48524772)**

If Trump succeeds in ending mail-in voting, seniors, rural voters, people with disabilities, military families, and so many others would be hit first and hardest. This isn’t about “election integrity,” it’s about control. Trump knows his agenda is unpopular, so he’s trying to shrink the electorate instead of winning it by gerrymandering districts and trying to end mail-in voting. It’s straight out of the authoritarian playbook. New Dems are fighting back in Congress, but we can’t do it alone. To stop this, we need to show overwhelming, undeniable public opposition to these attacks on our democracy. name: Case Dixon
subject: Door-knocking, Dixon For Congress
body:
Hey everyone,

As many of you may have seen, this past Thursday I officially qualified to be on the Republican ballot for the May 19th primary in congressional district 6.

[SEE HERE](https://alpolitics.com/case-dixon-qualifies-for-gop-primary-in-6th-district-challenge/ "https://alpolitics.com/case-dixon-qualifies-for-gop-primary-in-6th-district-challenge/")

As the election draws near, next week we'll be launching our door-knocking effort across the district. This is one of the most important things we’ll do between now and the primary, because grassroots campaigns are won face-to-face with voters.

I’m writing to ask for concrete commitments from those who can help. We’re building a core group of volunteers who can commit to one door-knocking shift per week, typically about 1.5–2 hours, either on a weeknight or Saturday. This will go on up to the May 19 primary. If you can commit, you will be making a real difference in the campaign. We need at least 10 people.

For those interested, please reply and let us know:
• which days work best for you
• whether you prefer weekdays or weekends

If a weekly commitment isn’t possible, that’s okay. We’ll also be organizing specific weekend door-knocking opportunities and clearly defined canvassing days where people can plug in as their schedules allow. Let us know if that’s a better fit, and we’ll keep you informed as those dates are scheduled.

We’ll make this easy and organized. The campaign will provide clear instructions on where to go, which houses to hit, and how to track your doors. No experience is necessary — we’ll walk you through everything and make sure you’re comfortable before you start.

This campaign is going to be won by people who are willing to show up and talk directly with voters. Your time, just like your support so far, truly matters.

Thank you for being part of this effort, and I hope you’ll consider joining us on the doors.

In Liberty,
**Case Dixon**
****
Dixon for Congress
(205) 282-9103
615 Brooklane Dr. #3218
Hueytown, AL 35023-2508
DIXONFORUSCONGRESS.COM

name: GOTV PAC
subject: Trump joked about canceling the 2026 election
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpac2026?refcode=Email1022026 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpac2026?refcode=Email1022026]
Peter ,
Donald Trump just “joked” about canceling the 2026 midterms.
He floated the idea in front of the entire House Republican Conference – before allegedly backpedaling and blaming the media. But when a man who already tried to overturn one election twice starts musing about canceling another, it’s not a joke. It’s a warning.
He’s already installed allies across the country. He’s fueling new voter suppression laws. And now he’s testing the waters on whether America will tolerate skipping the next election altogether.
We won’t.
That’s why we’re building the biggest Get Out The Vote operation in our history to protect democracy, register voters, and make sure turnout soars in 2026 – even if Trump and his allies try to stop it.
But we need your help: Can you chip in $25 today to help us fight Trump’s attacks on democracy and mobilize voters for 2026? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpac2026?refcode=Email1102026]

We can’t afford to sit this out. Trump already tried to end democracy once. This time, we stop him before he tries again.

name: Kyle Blomquist for Congress
subject: Dismantling ICE is now the moderate position
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blomquist for Congress
body:
ICE has become the President's own militarized, secret police force | | | Dismantling ICE is the moderate position. ICE has become the President's own militarized, secret police force Peter, ICE has become the President's own militarized, secret police force, deployed for strictly political purposes, without cause or consequence. It terrorizes our communities, tears apart our families, and murders civilians with impunity. Dismantling ICE is now the moderate position. **They will call us radical, but is what radical looks like:** Dozens of ICE agents storming a public library to detain one man. 44 violent incidents by ICE agents in the past year, including 16 shootings and 4 deaths. J.D. Vance holding a press conference to manufacture nonexistent legal principles like “absolute immunity” to defend a murderer, and saying the agents were "going door to door to try to find criminals". That is not a legitimate law enforcement operation. Or constitutional. Peter, abolishing ICE is both urgent and essential. We need leaders with the courage to say so and the conviction to do so. – Kyle If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: [Express Donate clean_body: ICE has become the President's own militarized, secret police force | | | Dismantling ICE is the moderate position. ICE has become the President's own militarized, secret police force Peter, ICE has become the President's own militarized, secret police force, deployed for strictly political purposes, without cause or consequence. It terrorizes our communities, tears apart our families, and murders civilians with impunity. Dismantling ICE is now the moderate position. They will call us radical, but is what radical looks like: Dozens of ICE agents storming a public library to detain one man. 44 violent incidents by ICE agents in the past year, including 16 shootings and 4 deaths. J.D. Vance holding a press conference to manufacture nonexistent legal principles like “absolute immunity” to defend a murderer, and saying the agents were "going door to door to try to find criminals". That is not a legitimate law enforcement operation. Or constitutional. Peter, abolishing ICE is both urgent and essential. We need leaders with the courage to say so and the conviction to do so. – Kyle If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Or mail a check to: P.O. Box 66, Iron Mountain, MI 49801 Paid for by Blomquist for Congress P.O. Box 66, Iron Mountain, MI 49801 Kyle Blomquist is running to represent the people of Michigan’s 1st District in the U.S. House of Representatives, on a mission to unify working people and dramatically improve their lives. | | Sent via . name: Janet Mills
subject: I looked Trump in the eyes…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
I’m not afraid of a bully

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)
| | |
---
[](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11564?t=2&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)
null – It’s Janet Mills, Governor of Maine.

Just months ago, I did something no one does: When Donald Trump tried to intimidate me and Maine people, I looked him in the eyes and told him _I’d see him in court._ And guess what? I did. **We took him to court – and won.**
I’m running for U.S. Senate to defeat Susan Collins because Maine deserves a senator who isn’t afraid of going toe-to-toe with a bully.

**Trump is putting Maine and our country in danger every single day, and Susan Collins is refusing to do anything about it.**
**[null, this is going to be one of the most expensive races in the country. MAGA Republicans will spend whatever it takes to defeat me. I’m ready to fight and win – but I can’t do it without your help. So please, split a donation of $10 or $15 bucks between my campaign and Haley Stevens' campaign, and let’s get to work>> ](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11564?t=3&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)**
| | [Donate $10](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11565?t=4&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)
---
| | [Donate $15](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11566?t=5&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)
---
| | [Donate Another Amount](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11564?t=6&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)
---
I love Maine with all that I am, null.

Trust me when I say I wouldn't be running otherwise.
**As Maine’s first woman District Attorney, Attorney General, and Governor – I’ve dedicated my life to this state. But when I was deciding what was next for me ... I knew I could never live with myself if I didn’t take on our biggest fight yet.
**
I know better than anyone how much more harm is going to fall on Maine people if we keep allowing Susan Collins to fail us.
What Donald Trump is doing is not business as usual. This moment is not a difference of political opinions – our country is at a tipping point that demands real leadership.

[**So please, split a donation between my campaign and Haley Stevens' campaign to help me show Donald Trump and Susan Collins that we’re ready to fight like hell to win in Maine and flip the Senate majority >> **](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11564?t=7&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Janet Mills and Haley Stevens will go through immediately:_ | | | [Donate $5](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11567?t=8&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)
---
| | [Donate $25](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11568?t=9&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)
---
| | [Donate $50](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11569?t=10&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)
---
| | [Donate Another Amount](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11564?t=11&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)
---
Together,

Janet Mills
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11599?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=12&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11571?t=13&akid=1884%2E49239%2EmZ7v2L)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley

body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23809%2E4449941%2EqIu1Nc) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23809%2E4449941%2EqIu1Nc) Team,

You probably get a lot of emails like this one, but I want to explain why [**I’m asking you to split a donation between my campaign and Fight For Reform and why it’s so important right now.**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71830?t=3&akid=23809%2E4449941%2EqIu1Nc)

2026 is an election year in Maryland — but for nearly _three months,_ starting on Wednesday, I won’t be allowed to raise a single dollar so that we can focus on the important work of driving progress forward for Marylanders.

That’s a real challenge for my team. These early months are when most campaigns build their momentum. It’s when they get the fuel for their organizing, advertising, and ability to define the race. [**And that’s what my Republican opponents will be doing the entire time my team has their hands tied.**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71830?t=4&akid=23809%2E4449941%2EqIu1Nc)

While we’re in the fundraising freeze, Republicans will spend every day raising money, meeting with Trump’s advisors, and collecting massive checks from special-interest donors who want to flip this seat.

That’s why the short time we have left is critical. We’ve got less than a week to bring in the resources we need to stay competitive. There’s no “catching up” later — only a sprint that starts right now.

[**If you can split a donation of $10, $25, or any amount between Fight For Reform and me before the clock runs out, it will help us fight against anything Republicans throw our way in 2026. Will you step up before it’s too late?**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71830?t=5&akid=23809%2E4449941%2EqIu1Nc) | | [DONATE $5 NOW >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71831?amount=5&amounts=25%2C50%2C100%2C250&t=6&akid=23809%2E4449941%2EqIu1Nc)

clean_body: | Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending Citizens United state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, If you would like to unsubscribe, Team, You probably get a lot of emails like this one, but I want to explain why 2026 is an election year in Maryland — but for nearly three months, starting on Wednesday, I won’t be allowed to raise a single dollar so that we can focus on the important work of driving progress forward for Marylanders. That’s a real challenge for my team. These early months are when most campaigns build their momentum. It’s when they get the fuel for their organizing, advertising, and ability to define the race. While we’re in the fundraising freeze, Republicans will spend every day raising money, meeting with Trump’s advisors, and collecting massive checks from special-interest donors who want to flip this seat. That’s why the short time we have left is critical. We’ve got less than a week to bring in the resources we need to stay competitive. There’s no “catching up” later — only a sprint that starts right now. | | | | | | | Let’s cross the finish line together, team. Elevate, Wes | This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com | | Paid political advertisement paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P.O. Box 66005, Washington, D.C. 20035. Authorized by Wes Moore for Maryland. Contributions to End Citizens United Non-Federal are not tax deductible. Contributions to End Citizens United Non-federal will be used in End Citizens United Non-federal’s sole discretion and are not being earmarked for use in any particular state or election.

name: DNC HQ
subject: $7 vs $300 million
body:
---
Derek, Donald Trump’s super PAC recently announced that they have more than _$300 million_ in funds ahead of the 2026 midterms. So today, we’re hoping for a large number of individual donations as we prepare to go toe-to-toe with Trump and his allies in the midterms. This is not just about raising money for the sake of it — it's about building the long-term infrastructure we’ll need to elect Democrats in the midterms and beyond. That’s why today, we’re asking for $7. $7 to help take back the House and Senate. $7 to stop Trump’s disastrous agenda in its tracks. $7 because the DNC is the organization best positioned to move resources where they are needed most — and that’s exactly what we’re focused on right now. So far this year, thousands of Democrats have already chipped in to help us go up against Trump’s $300 million machine. Today’s the day they’re hoping you’ll join them. [**Please contribute $7 or whatever amount makes sense for you to the Democratic National Committee. Any amount will go a long way, because it adds up quickly when everyone chips in.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?refcode=em_20260110_5731_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5731_22930333_cQpO8M&akid=5731%2E22930333%2EcQpO8M) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260110_5731_fr&refcode2=5731_22930333_cQpO8M&akid=5731%2E22930333%2EcQpO8M)**

name: Frank S. Wright - Corporal, U.S. Marine Corps (Ret.)
subject: At 100 years old, I’m still serving my country
body:
Two wounds, a lifetime of gratitude
I still remember seeing the flag raised on Iwo Jima...

| | | [](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=06995171cfa246509558ace1ae7213b4&_e=j-FboOvI3TKNadd_VYYRuYS0M4brCK9GFF4CFD5XxEwgLnZDr7Qe-3eUPyxu8dplTPmE63Ha_355HSAKIXQ0IGIg2VZlE5UhYrIdPbN6_rA7JPOGl5CAgaWUmx_cN1VUyqQ20slcglgKIWvON0oRR82jP0sg7OWhR0AZcBsg6xHJ__JhfVFkvx_OsKJzAdeh9xN0ZwiNG8Ox7NWb6gCVo-xWxMIV8gxK23RPRC8xzegduMIzTZaMfnB4J-mkT52U_D4hqM0dNl4SlR96Hgp2NqmJQc0sCO25eDmAa2nguGRrcP6Ksirx3prlsavFjGPUL3D1QVd_1GO_vhtF4KZcwIZsrDAP2RBMugbgKn0p-1LJe_7ZPXYymQUexHQ3KgpQHP5kxiMsLGsPW9o6BGGV9bUrp7QqPy9mk-lwhnQwkR-s44b21bEvzYBs-EWHclmJohHGu2wo57TAw4Co_BfaNZPRYVqlp9DXJP8UsBLOtp5NhKmptoLRXJNbuzAIaXyzDW2glKcW3wuddjcRBRGwyZ46Mb29XDA3U5rih3qBYhNmCKJnepSYibUfQvwODK0E7x6bxMgDiC_RB2tjSvpyBBn4NEF2Bk-HhpKFaDEnQzg9wwzkhSLJXKyoR6OX65N81bLl3CVz0BWYWvKRYQvLwu3D7MgLiDnNQCLbs-rPzf8%3D)
---
Fellow Patriot,
---
My name is **Corporal Frank S. Wright** , United States Marine Corps, retired. I'm a hundred years old now -- one of the last living Marine Raiders of World War II.In January 1942, just weeks after the attack on Pearl Harbor, I quit high school in Arkansas and walked into the enlistment office. I was sixteen -- too young, too skinny, and too light to qualify. The sergeant told me to come back when I was heavier.So I went home, ate all the bananas I could find, and stuffed five pounds' worth into my pockets before stepping on that scale again.I passed. I lied about my age. And I became a Marine.
---
[](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=06995171cfa246509558ace1ae7213b4&_e=j-FboOvI3TKNadd_VYYRuYS0M4brCK9GFF4CFD5XxEwgLnZDr7Qe-3eUPyxu8dplTPmE63Ha_355HSAKIXQ0IGIg2VZlE5UhYrIdPbN6_rA7JPOGl5CAgaWUmx_cN1VUyqQ20slcglgKIWvON0oRR82jP0sg7OWhR0AZcBsg6xHJ__JhfVFkvx_OsKJzAdeh9xN0ZwiNG8Ox7NWb6gCVo-xWxMIV8gxK23RPRC8xzejZbYPVuWFneBaGuj17bjBVl-PKT8yIMcT5UsC0UlourwRFuvE1ItDawL5Mzo7XjxbELuV9C_J8JTequUaFy7MKD9ASNP6EBenSsiN_y3oV4tbWekR6f48d97uFtKsOWOIpJC1qAblTuwHmMtw4yj939QV-e2hfvvNGuxCAmRlvDB9GNFTw4xYMA8jiGASpQHVf0YDRWWXd8D5yYu_h33htFeNxaDuE7ZAQBPqrLdI_V44m1z70BUxga8XBow_Jw5T4Cm6_VSVHS9NUjKzYG9J1fUE0q0o_2lAIlge79ICR-xE4vtcapaEJ_2p97R1SX1PH6t17qKvQkmShafGvrNxB8Gb8eFFYPRd_8pRixHweSPtBzHRC53rEQCdlcG2zH0jOcR4bdBYF3MdoqcYVp9WL0KbWDTfd_5wXPIiDq8UvZ2deP7j-WZS1d1OJzBEaOx4%3D)
---
After boot camp, I volunteered to join the **4th Marine Raiders** , America's first special operations unit.They asked me why I wanted to join. I said, _" Because I want to fight."_Our commanding officer was **James Roosevelt** , the President's son -- a good man, the kind who never gave an order he wouldn't do himself. We were trained to land first, fight behind enemy lines, and take the beaches nobody else could reach.From **Guadalcanal** to **New Georgia** , from the jungles of the Solomons to the black sand of **Iwo Jima** , we fought to free the Pacific.On **Guam** , I was stabbed in the stomach during hand-to-hand combat. On **Iwo Jima** , I was hit by machine-gun fire and knocked unconscious. Both times, my men thought I was gone. Somehow, I wasn't.When I came to, I saw the flag rising on Mount Suribachi. Every ship in the harbor sounded its horn. I had tears in my eyes then, and I still do when I think about it.That flag meant freedom still lived.
---
[](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=06995171cfa246509558ace1ae7213b4&_e=j-FboOvI3TKNadd_VYYRuYS0M4brCK9GFF4CFD5XxEwgLnZDr7Qe-3eUPyxu8dplTPmE63Ha_355HSAKIXQ0IGIg2VZlE5UhYrIdPbN6_rA7JPOGl5CAgaWUmx_cN1VUyqQ20slcglgKIWvON0oRR82jP0sg7OWhR0AZcBsg6xHJ__JhfVFkvx_OsKJzAdeh9xN0ZwiNG8Ox7NWb6gCVo-xWxMIV8gxK23RPRC8xzegaxjOQp25WlsBXesGJhMRYxz5Zc3OL504FrklrYd08STul_yFAY29ysFGS7LPBVSbPAJcacRE3ws8X_z8uEeMJEFm4BUPBVPj0L3vYjBmctbB1sKYf4iJNVAa3zVxn_qmioozVeyyAcORjcyI1V1nPUBxDJ9Ddt1hxMWrgn-VBctaEy4avkHR3aIM9Q1igkxnLN8hE1qSTZlYvTh5yidnQ8yWXn5i9zHFXErw9ouBNwYfluiTyqnetHbt0u1Dk6Ri-iz5dlht5Brt876GEQcbmSbAIrRd3mvnU3nu6DVwNHKRXy4pS3t-XOiqqugg7Aa0-GR3xVVxkyyeStDyhS5fSxqeHj041DRZhISGkPomTcT7Mf9LlwV0VJvUxLfAEbI_SADA4SppJtV16iFxfvqJmyln9wJiP3wATSGVZ1YIpN3SHB8FLU69mqJr-4rH74I0%3D)
---
There were dark days too...On Guadalcanal, I caught malaria and ended up in a field hospital -- but when my unit was preparing to move out, I couldn't stand being left behind.I slipped out and rejoined them. I'll now admit, I was always a little rebellious. When the war ended, I became a drill instructor, helping shape the next generation of Marines. I was in my twenties, carrying wounds inside and out.For more than a decade, I didn't share my stories. I kept them locked away, like many of my brothers did. Over the years, I began to open up and share my story with friends, family, and others who wanted to know about the realities of the battlefield at the Pacific Front.Now, more than ever, the **American Veterans Center** has given me the opportunity to share them -- on the stage at the **Annual Conference** , and by capturing them on film for future generations. Not just my story, but those of the men who never came home, and those who are no longer here to tell theirs.
---

clean_body: Two wounds, a lifetime of gratitude I still remember seeing the flag raised on Iwo Jima... | | | Fellow Patriot, My name is Corporal Frank S. Wright , United States Marine Corps, retired. I'm a hundred years old now -- one of the last living Marine Raiders of World War II.In January 1942, just weeks after the attack on Pearl Harbor, I quit high school in Arkansas and walked into the enlistment office. I was sixteen -- too young, too skinny, and too light to qualify. The sergeant told me to come back when I was heavier.So I went home, ate all the bananas I could find, and stuffed five pounds' worth into my pockets before stepping on that scale again.I passed. I lied about my age. And I became a Marine. After boot camp, I volunteered to join the 4th Marine Raiders , America's first special operations unit.They asked me why I wanted to join. I said, " Because I want to fight."Our commanding officer was James Roosevelt , the President's son -- a good man, the kind who never gave an order he wouldn't do himself. We were trained to land first, fight behind enemy lines, and take the beaches nobody else could reach.From Guadalcanal to New Georgia , from the jungles of the Solomons to the black sand of Iwo Jima , we fought to free the Pacific.On Guam , I was stabbed in the stomach during hand-to-hand combat. On Iwo Jima , I was hit by machine-gun fire and knocked unconscious. Both times, my men thought I was gone. Somehow, I wasn't.When I came to, I saw the flag rising on Mount Suribachi. Every ship in the harbor sounded its horn. I had tears in my eyes then, and I still do when I think about it.That flag meant freedom still lived. There were dark days too...On Guadalcanal, I caught malaria and ended up in a field hospital -- but when my unit was preparing to move out, I couldn't stand being left behind.I slipped out and rejoined them. I'll now admit, I was always a little rebellious. When the war ended, I became a drill instructor, helping shape the next generation of Marines. I was in my twenties, carrying wounds inside and out.For more than a decade, I didn't share my stories. I kept them locked away, like many of my brothers did. Over the years, I began to open up and share my story with friends, family, and others who wanted to know about the realities of the battlefield at the Pacific Front.Now, more than ever, the American Veterans Center has given me the opportunity to share them -- on the stage at the Annual Conference , and by capturing them on film for future generations. Not just my story, but those of the men who never came home, and those who are no longer here to tell theirs. They've given old Marines like me a place to share what we lived through, to look young cadets and future service members in the eye and say, "This is what it means to serve."They've brought veterans from every generation together -- to remember, to teach, and to inspire. And every year, the American Veterans Center Annual Conference gives us something rare -- a gathering where history stands shoulder to shoulder with the future.It's where I've met cadets who listen with the same wide eyes I once had at sixteen. It's where old comrades find each other again, and where we all remember that the values we fought for -- courage, duty, honor -- are still being carried forward.It's a living bridge between those who fought for freedom and those who will defend it next. The National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C., where America gathers to honor its heroes. The Annual Veterans Conference each November, where we share stories and pass the torch to those who will serve next. The preservation of our stories through documentaries and oral histories -- so that when we are gone, the lessons remain. At my age, I don't take many things for granted. But I still believe in this country -- in what it stands for, and in the people who keep its history alive.If you believe that too, I ask you to help the American Veterans Center continue its work. So that long after the last of us is gone, our voices will still be heard -- reminders of what America can be when its people stand together.Thank you for remembering. Thank you for caring. And thank you for keeping faith with those who served.Semper Fidelis, Frank S. Wright Corporal, U.S. Marine Corps (Ret.) Veteran, 4th Marine Raiders | 21st Marines, 3rd Marine Division P.S.** When I was sixteen, I had to lie about my age to fight for my country. Now, at one hundred, I can tell you the truth as plain as I know it: freedom isn't free, and the stories of those who fought for it are fading fast. Your gift -- whatever you can give -- helps make sure they're never forgotten. The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. (C) American Veterans Center 1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201

_**EXHIBIT 1 - JOINT FUNDRAISING NOTICE **_

Team Nunn ("JFC") is a joint fundraising committee composed of Iowans for Zach Nunn ("Campaign"), Call to Service PAC ("LPAC") and NRCC (each, a "Committee," and, collectively, the "Committees"). Any contribution to the JFC permissible under the Federal Election Campaign Act, as amended ("FECA"), from contributors who have not exceeded their applicable federal contribution limits, shall be allocated among the Committees pursuant to FECA and the formula set forth in this Notice.

_**Contributions Made by an Individual or Federal Non-Multicandidate Political Committee:**_
Individuals or federal non-multicandidate PACs may contribute up to $322,100. Such contributions shall be allocated in the following order:
1\. Campaign shall receive any contribution not exceeding $7,000, designating up to $3,500 to the primary election and up to $3,500 to the general election, provided such limits apply.
2\. LPAC shall receive any contribution not exceeding $5,000 annually.
3\. NRCC shall receive for its general account any contribution not exceeding $44,300 annually.
4\. NRCC shall receive any contribution not to exceed $132,900 for its Building Fund, provided such limits apply.
5\. NRCC shall receive any contribution not to exceed $132,900 for its Legal Proceedings Fund, provided such limits apply.

_**Contributions Made by a Federal Multicandidate Political Committee:**_
Multicandidate PACs may contribute up to $120,000. Such contributions shall be allocated in the following order:
1\. Campaign shall receive any contribution not exceeding $10,000, designating up to $5,000 to the primary election and up to $5,000 to the general election, provided such limits apply.
2\. LPAC shall receive any contribution not exceeding $5,000 annually.
3 .NRCC shall receive for its general account any contribution not exceeding $15,000 annually.
4\. NRCC shall receive any contribution not to exceed $45,000 for its Building Fund, provided such limits apply.

name: Abdul El-Sayed
subject: In 8-in-10 elections, the candidate who spends the most money wins
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abdul for U
body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1540%2E251025%2ElBTY0j ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1540%2E251025%2ElBTY0j ]Abdul for US Senate

Peter,

I want to be honest with you about something that keeps me up at night.

It’s the fact that our message doesn’t mean a damn thing if people never
hear it.

We can have the best ideas in the world. We can be right on the issues. We
can stand for working people instead of corporations.

But if we can’t get that message in front of voters, we lose.

That’s the hard truth of modern campaigns. In 8-in-10 elections, the
candidate who spends the most money wins.

My opponents are backed by corporate donors and groups like AIPAC, who can
flip a switch and drop millions of dollars on ads whenever they want. I
don’t have that luxury, by design.

I chose not to take corporate PAC or AIPAC money because I don’t believe
billionaires should decide our future. The working people who built this
country should.

We can win this race. I know that. The polls show it. The reason why we’re
a real contender in this competitive primary is because people like you
have stepped up.

However, if we fall behind on fundraising, we can’t run the ads, hire the
organizers, or counter the attacks our opponents are preparing. It greatly
impacts our odds of winning.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20260110-1-ak.QDR213&amount=10&refcode=em20260110-1535&t=1004&akid=1540%2E251025%2ElBTY0j ]So if you believe in a government that works for working people, not
billionaires or corporations, can you pitch in $10 today to
help us get the word out?

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20260110-1-ak.QDR213&amount=10&refcode=em20260110-1535&t=1023&akid=1540%2E251025%2ElBTY0j ]Donate $10 ››

Thank you for being in this fight with me.

Abdul

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. To contribute via check, please address it to Abdul for U.S. Senate, PO Box 126, St. Clair Shores, MI 48080.

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.abdulformichigan.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=1024&akid=1540%2E251025%2ElBTY0j
Update Your Information: https://act.abdulformichigan.com/signup/update-your-information
Unsubscribe: https://act.abdulformichigan.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1025&akid=1540%2E251025%2ElBTY0j

Email us: info@abdulformichigan.com

Paid for by Abdul for U.S. Senate

If there's anyone who has a record worthy of this, it's you. First of all, Congratulations! This is reserved only for top supporters of our movement. But I have to say, you've been so outstanding in your record of support that you've built up a profile that falls within the TOP Tier of GOP Patriots. one of House GOP's most trusted patriots, and you deserve to have the status to reflect that! And before you click, I want to let you know that we'll be leaning on you for your opinion on matters as we move forward. I'm putting this deal on the table because we can't afford to lose our most trusted allies when our majority is as thin as it is right now! You, God Bless You and God Bless America! name: The PreBorn! Team
subject: She came looking for help You gave her hope
body:
Every day, women across America search online for answers.

| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zai4aln4v8vj6ndo4dj81ajdcu20ggbaiiof957o_rp22sh2s8i6eor0shh69oj6cph2bs)
---
Hi Advocate for Life, Every day, women across America search online for answers. They are scared, uncertain, and convinced abortion is their only option. That’s where **PreBorn!** steps in. Our Network Clinics across the nation provide **free ultrasounds** , **holistic** **compassionate care** , and the **gospel of Jesus Christ** to women in crisis. Because when a mother sees her baby for the first time, when she hears that heartbeat, everything changes. In fact, 81% of women who receive an ultrasound, can see their baby and hear the heartbeat, will choose life for their babies. And your **$28 gift provides one life-saving ultrasound** and gives her the chance to meet her baby and hear of God’s love for her. As our thank-you, we’ll send you PreBorn!’s [**_Tiny Miracles, Mighty Futures_ 2026**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zsp3l0i6gb5qnqr3tg3ljbbhkuocvuihkqliomn0_rp22sh2s8i6eor0shh69oj6cph2bs) Calendar — a celebration of the lives your gift helps save and the futures your faith helps build. Inside, you’ll also find a **monthly prayer guide** to keep you connected to this mission all year long.
---
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zmlphkbtd1mk262oj748knn2a2dh36dh4vha2jto_rp22sh2s8i6eor0shh69oj6cph2bs)
---
Supplies are limited so give today to help reach the next mother before she’s too late to hear her baby’s heartbeat.
---
[**Give $28 to Save 1 Baby + Get Your Calendar** >>](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zloa03ep1ck0r5mtoaa2amnsik88tvtcdutj3ds0_rp22sh2s8i6eor0shh69oj6cph2bs)
---
Thank you for giving hope where it’s needed most. Gratefully, The PreBorn! Team
---

# **[The ticking clock no conservative wants to admit about 2026 midterms](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
Conservatives across the nation are already fretting over 2026’s midterm elections, convinced that a Democrat wave would tie the Trump administration’s hands for the president’s final two years.But BlazeTV hosts Steve Deace and Daniel Horowitz argue that’s the wrong mindset entirely. Rather than obsessing over winning elections they argue Democrats will almost certainly take, Republicans instead must be laser-focused on enacting permanent, fortress-like reforms right now — while they still hold power — before the window slams shut.“Will we jam through what it is we came to achieve — enduring victories — and meet the moment before that door slams?” asks Horowitz.
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger.)

name: Steve Scalise
subject: I am sorry Really I am
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
I completely understand how you feel because I would feel the same way.

I am genuinely sorry, Friend.
Really, I am.
I am sorry some folks threaten to remove you from the GOP if you don't donate.
I am sorry some folks masquerade as President Trump to try and fool you.
I am sorry some folks don't even thank you for donating.
It is enough to drive you nuts. And I completely understand how you feel because I get the same messages. I'll get bombarded with texts and emails asking me for money but it's hard to tell who sends them all.
And if I do pitch in a few bucks, I'd probably get pestered even more - without any acknowledgement of my contribution.
It's wrong, and I'm doing things differently.
This is Steve Scalise. First of all, I want to personally thank you for everything you've done to help elect President Trump and Republicans thus far. [You are the lifeblood of the MAGA movement - and we only won with your hard work and support.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zrlvr8t65n64i0n7i36vvs88abrja70mmd3flsa0_rp22sh2s8i6eor0shj64o76cjh2bs)
Thank you for having the grit and determination to stand up to the Democrat Machine at the height of its power.
Unfortunately, the Democrats have regrouped faster than anyone thought possible.
They're ALREADY unloading 50 million dollars into enough battleground districts to flip the House. They're ALREADY dragging President Trump into court.
[In other words, we really need some more support right now to keep fighting.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zm8edq1e69bc7vi7s7okj7qehjj3i3ka2jooj98o_rp22sh2s8i6eor0shj64o76cjh2bs)
If you can't afford to pitch right now, that's okay. I won't hold it against you. Take care of your family first. I won't question your Party loyalty or shame you for not donating.
BUT if you can contribute - even if it's just a few bucks - it would go a long way. Especially right now.
We have no choice but to hit our upcoming fundraising goal. Because if we fail, the Democrat Machine will crush us in every battleground district, barge back into power, and effectively BLOCKADE President Trump's entire legislative agenda.
[So I am sorry I even have to ask you this, but can you please pitch in before our BIGGEST deadline of the year?](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zog5fmu2c8lmt4r8kss1bqedjbhufd4vbpuok5e8_rp22sh2s8i6eor0shj64o76cjh2bs)
It would mean a great deal to me personally if you'd be willing to help.[IF YOU CAN, PLEASE PITCH IN HERE
](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zaba42gk0qirbu1go24b0i8831r1rlqh88bulfjo_rp22sh2s8i6eor0shj64o76cjh2bs)Either way, thanks for all you do. I mean that from the bottom of my heart.
God bless you, - Steve
STEVE SCALISEHouse Majority Leader

* * *

NOW: Republicans are leading the way to take back our land![](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2ztrb8pfleebv6tvjj4i3ebjgrs5jgse7ftkuqa38_rp22sh2s8i6eor0shj64o76cjh2bs)[Sign our petition right here and right now to force Chinese nationals to liquidate all U.S. land and help Republicans save America's heartland!](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zlq3oohhr8ct8k6hj3g24dqiei517l09vreenrgo_rp22sh2s8i6eor0shj64o76cjh2bs)[SIGN THE PETITION

Far too many Americans do not know the meaning and purpose of the U.S. Constitution.

# IN YOUR WEEKLY BULLETIN:
---
|
---
| |
When institutions fail, the consequences are never abstract. This week’s _Sunday Bulletin_ examines what happens when warnings go unheeded, accountability is delayed, and the public is left to absorb the fallout. From a deadly explosion in Bucks County to the slow erosion of local journalism, these are stories about responsibility — and what happens when it’s absent.
**INVESTIGATIVE:** A third lawsuit has been filed in connection with the Dec. 23 explosion at a Bucks County nursing home, alleging a gas leak was known, ignored, and allowed to “fester for days.” As investigations continue, the legal filings paint a stark picture of institutional neglect — and raise urgent questions about who is responsible when vulnerable populations are left unprotected.
**POLITICS:** Senator John Fetterman’s transformation from progressive favorite to political outlier continues to reshape Pennsylvania’s political landscape. As he breaks with his party on borders, foreign policy, and government shutdowns, Fetterman has gained unexpected support — and sharpened divisions inside his own coalition.
**REGIONAL NEWS:** A dramatic rescue in Atlantic City turns violent. A closely divided borough council reveals fractures in local leadership. And a Philadelphia family welcomes one of the region’s first babies of 2026 — reminders that community life continues amid crisis, conflict, and change.
**FROM THE EDITORS:** The impending shutdown of the _Pittsburgh Post-Gazette_ marks more than the loss of a newspaper. It signals a dangerous moment for civic life in Pennsylvania — and raises a fundamental question: can a functioning republic survive without a functioning news media?
**OPINION & ANALYSIS: **From rising health-care costs and Obamacare subsidies to reflections on conscience, faith, and dissent, our columnists take on the debates shaping public trust — and challenge readers to look beyond slogans to consequences.
---

# Sunday, January 11, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in**** 1908, President Theodore Roosevelt used the Antiquities Act to designate[ the Grand Canyon as a national monument](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u5CAQhE9jZ1iA_wOCTZy9TTe0eqA95i2mLegZi9uvPNobvEq_UulT_flav8AHH5-_4UDDG60RrxyQGdPqLZ6JGDPnV3Z4Bir5KJlx1VIPQiqh1OrAh7J2tTMbosa2RqPGYVJ67vu53g12GlqLcnJ2friH3LR7jJuCqXXOqnGovbnXpLoj-35stBxA2XGcZjvM3TZWnUTnmVLzTT7yjlso9MbUWDrqYHbmM1ftr0ovlV6u62p2n5lSuXmlF959Fg6K8FH8J5VeviG-IBWh1KeC5CihSEQZ3xhYHPAXs3gmiE5YiIWiABGBPUUI4qD4OjBynYw7Lx-Cz8Pt-TzAh48X_-jMt9H_AgAA__-BdI1c), preserving its iconic landscape while acknowledging its natural beauty and cultural significance. Today's weeknight family dinners often emphasize fresh, healthy ingredients. But previous generations prepared suppers around unglamorous pantry staples that were meant to stretch the budget, as recalled in today's _[Whatever Happened To...](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOszAQBOCngQ5kb4INhYu_ocvfXokcewl7MjZiN0G8_Yl7hZt69I00X4_p4SlRfv33KzqZy5Tx4IQiuE8UcNuLIAu_OeKWysnryYITKDCN0o3WU_SUzuleRzcjAt5qdNqaXsPQdUO9OKtMr3WIT-z1M6i-6yP4OBiw3kDUtiZ3aUpfUV1nW1DG62BtPwQz3Gdb3RVGkrK334WyLDins3xwb0NZ6-QWkY2r278KxgrG4zha3jDHg2TZMGdCvooVjOj5bOSdfRM8M-4lYQVjvbu4HZQSsbmmXqun9EvLn_74OPgJAAD__3zYdaw)_ segment. If you've been concerned about your cholesterol numbers or heart health as you get older, today's sponsor, [Cholesterol Conquered](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkb1u3DAMgJ9G3nzQv86DhgLBbenaUdBRdKxWtlyJl8BvXzi9oWOBcCNBfCQ__ngNrzGXvL19jyt6mmvY8KMXJMIWMuDeKmGn_ugJ91KPvh6dMEgu7cjFKERIMZcj6CH5GVGiGtALZ69CTsZMw-IFAmhtBAehjBN3QIXoUNkorVHghuxPGhdncGPcRXIbBTh3ncBOenZMc0yZarv8rHmjBedy1HdsF6jrUPxCtHemvjF5Y_JGLcKvCyy1YCdstUDdfj-wYTrbmbxhNHfg7j5OSuhRg01jNAlHx6-OX2ctpUpM3fYSAVfciKmX57zQ97r12sIz_8fUub20TNqYcmLqRcztkSksgklLn5WUGwLdw_-ywl_E0HzaP3IpudtTxNsac_k8nL70rXcv_wQAAP__HK-sQQ), is sharing a surprising, doctor-backed discovery you'll want to see.

## Weekend Anti-ICE Protests Set Nationwide
Over [1,000 anti-ICE protests and vigils](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMuupCAQhp8GdxjAC7pg0ZOJu57MYi5Lg1Aq0wiGotv49hM7OTkPcGpTi0r9-b_v7328a-ddWH7oDVSe4xjgQA85QxqdgT3FDJjxiRZ2H0_cTswwCiZayjjlfLTa-XOsC6tmAAFVAYrLtuOib5q-WJWd5dSxWhrD-NT2XW0nq6uulrqvmWxE4dSVxvg1rGlkKViruZGy603b17MkNQPrckzlv-hCXmH2Z3xBKk3cCq_WnHck1Y2IgYjhOI5yw3DdiBgg0CcSMVxQn8sZoDpYOsVkIdFd5xQ9xTXG7MKCFHedHnR9BpvAIo0zPQAeECx9ucV5fD9_mCFi0InebvzXb0w_STVE4yypvn-7pMKBfyDtRVJ2P5z3DtsLZ9m08-_6-UvOX0r8DwAA__-6OJjN) are happening nationwide after Renee Nicole Good was fatally shot during an ICE enforcement operation on Wednesday in Minneapolis. [A second shooting](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uhDAMgJ8mbKDEByEMGbpku64dUUrM4TYQRHyHePsqlfoE9WAv_vk-f9zHu6dI2-Pdr2h5TuOGZ47IjMdIE-5HYsycnzngHtOV1yszjiBB11LVSo3BU7zGtgp2RgS8VWhVr42CoeuGarE3KZVvIYTOqNb0xnxOgDroWYbgJ68rsmWbVCVk1_UNSO3V1PdmmPTQzr1oJQbidDRfiTZecI5XeuHRTGmtol2Y9yxubwKcAHeeZ_PMnlPwV2kQ4DKn4xLgilkpniltAlw5K8BJVdJQZhc8sPYbU00T1n_uNS-U6xPxG7cgwBkjB6m1lrIX4KrDhv2kGCnrgvpYPcVfNP7XP18WfgIAAP__S7aHwg) by a border patrol agent of a married couple in Portland, Oregon, on Jan. 8 also prompted the protests. Minneapolis police on Saturday said [at least 30 people were detained](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO4yAQhJ8GOiLYYAwFxTXucu2VFrHXYU_YWGYvkd_-5F_6n-CfdqSZ-ebPY3wkKrS9fqcVIy913PDTCjLjMdKE-1EZG7d_bca91LOtZ2McQYNT2ihjxjlROUcr57ggAt4lRtM7byB0XZA5Guy9ddokl6xPdrY-3MMzBWvCE5wLkuKVps0l3XX9DbRLZup7HyYX7NILq3Emrsftb6WNMy7lrG88blNdZYmZeW_i_kvAIGDgjJlKuTwBQ64rXkAChsaJUX0ST1nA0DkP3ga10rZh2muhpr5ZFU2oWq6VaXsJGOQR5_1DpVBz15jXmuirQPKPHntH-B8AAP__nsKAKQ) during protests on Friday night. Demonstrators reportedly threw snow, ice, and rocks at officers, police vehicles, and other vehicles. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem said Good, a 37-year-old U.S. citizen, was ["stalking and impeding agents"](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1uhTAQhE8DnZFt_gsXaehe2pTIMWvYyHiRve8RcvqIXCFbbDOa0Tfz8ZgfFgPG9d3uYNjTHOHMAZghzejgSMSQOT_zAkegK-9XZpi11J2QSig1LxbDNTflYjyAhroEo_puUHps27HczODavoWm93qR0o21axo31t2glf700NkSzZ0m1X2ybftKy84q1_fD6Lqx8X3RSFiQKVVfhJE38OGiF6TK0V4GszEfuajfCj0VejrPs_L0fbe49UJPz1zoaccYwR4UMIv7OxCRzg0SCE_PuAi7IlumlEUG_AHhKHKiIDInABbWMyRx2_JGxBjXMpnlODEEzN2NuO4Wwx8S_2vHl9G_AQAA__8hMot-) and was instructed to get out of her car just before the shooting, but did not comply. Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz authorized the Minnesota National Guard to be ready to support local and state law enforcement to keep protests peaceful.
## Trump Calls For Cap on Credit Card Rates
President Trump on Friday called for [a one-year cap on credit card interest rates](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O5CAQhJ8GZ1jQY_wTEFzibC690OKa9pgVBgt6xvLbr7yvsJWW9NWn-vdcni7EkF5_3U6W17wkOmskZipLQDpKZqpc39XTEfNV96syLaCgl0pLrRfvQryWrvF2JQJ6NGT10I8aJmOmZrMjDgr71RgY1eSMccZ13eShB0KPEzbB3jSl7yhjhhZU7zQOwzhhP3XrIDpFPnAu7VcOiTda45U_VFrMexPtxnxU8fgjYBYwn-fZYvqPdylgvtECZqUFzFoJmLm890Oii7HKNReZE8mLXJHoDpmTxHJvSXTFy5CYClWWxTFV6VhqdVBBStxuvMemWH-cIcZQ-9vytbsQf6z4V1d-LHwHAAD__x0wiE8) at 10% beginning on Jan. 20. Lawmakers from both the Democrat and Republican parties have raised concerns about high rates and have called for them to be addressed. Americans are paying, on average, [between 19.65% and 21.5% in interest](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1uwzAMhU9jbwos2pasQUOXbOna0aAlOlEr_0BkEvj2hXuFciMe8OF77-s23jDltN4_cSEv8zau9OZMIlTGFGgvmxALPznSnreDl4OFRmjAqEYrrceIKR9jV0c_EwG1NXltzaDB9b2rH74dUIMDG51ptYmhdXaODXZIFLsYqU7-pDX6vKbv7QUagzpYO7hgXDfbqmsoJtnK5XtLqzxozsf2onIJ21Jn_xDZuWo_KrhWcMX9LHBG51MkhUwVXKU8l12FcoJUwBJZpVWoEIsqKMSK8ZXWO6sJ1x9WcUIAPWsAO7gObNDBQD_hYNvgJqvr4uP-TjknNqfgfcGU_4TkXyu-PPwGAAD__6TyhsI) on credit cards, according to the Federal Reserve and other industry tracking sources. Trump pushed for such a cap during the 2024 campaign, as [American credit card debt](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuhDAQRL_GdEZ4OTAULtLQXdqUaGUvh08GI3s5xN9Hzi9k6tG8mfl5zk_0we-vb9zI8BLnna4ciJnS7C0dKTJlzmd2dIR45-3OTDM00MtGSaVmhz7c86NyZiECaisySveDgrHrxmo12naIXYvg1GDV0rdDq_UyIikH0PZQeVPSGlXUdJ2uoelRWa2H0fbjY9Hi0ZDzHFP9jn7nlZZwxw-l2satCmZlPrJovwRMAqbrumpe6XVich734hEwnVmWXQKmwhIwvXEXMKlGwMTp3A5pU2FIi8lJvzMlyiwTMkmLR5WMOy4fgs99qfPa0Ic_PP_rs4-B3wAAAP__pcSAcg) hit a record of more than $1.1 trillion. The President was not clear in his social media post whether a cap might take effect through [executive action or legislation](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOtCAUhZ9GOwxcBbSg-Bu7-dstDcplZINC4M5MfPuN-wp76pPz5Xxfj-VhQwzn87890JBPy4mfGpEIyxI2zCURVqqv6jDHdNXjqoQLcFCMCybE4myI1zK0znhEwL5FI7QaBUxSTu1ueq_6deRWSo2Wa9BOrlJyJ9cRveZrG8y9xsUdLqXugCsrNq3HaVPT4HUzcHSBUum-UzhpRx-v9MbSbeloo9mJcm36fw3MDcw-nPbcsLvsntLdaGC-LzUwU3kdmeVX3bEywQRnm81MqJ4PoHTf7XTEthiXPyHGUNUNfh42xF8Q_cnO28BPAAAA___sV3gF).
## List: Best Housing Markets for Retirees
A nationwide housing market study ranked Saginaw, Michigan, as [the strongest housing market](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGunTAQBNCvMZ2RbcBcChdp6F7alMjAGjbP2Mi79yL-PiK_8KYejY7mz9f05TFi2n77AxyHPCW4KAIzlAkXOEtmIKY3rXDGfNNxE8NklLFSaan1tHqM99RWqwsABpoKnO7tS5uh64Zqd503uunM0KjOzMraYQ3LDHoOKvS2ta8K3bOm9BPVdX1tlPV66fvXsNihDb1oFazIudR_MybeIcQ7f6DUSz6q6Hbmk0TzS5hRmPG6rnrLs0_fmLbiGeipCTNi-gAxpk2YsYCPEog9gzAj7yBnIJY-rfLKhVju-U2YNnn48g1MMuQiCzAWAJKY5CMWZqyKW88LY0SyD3M7PMb_LP7Rlx9n_gUAAP__LJOKmg) for retirees in 2026. The study measured forecasted home values, percentages of homes with retirement income, and income required to afford a new home for the [6.5 million Americans](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGunTAQBNCvMZ2RbcBcChdp6F7alMjAGjbP2Mi79yL-PiK_8KYejY7mz9f05TFi2n77AxyHPCW4KAIzlAkXOEtmIKY3rXDGfNNxE8NklLFSaan1tHqM99RWqwsABpoKnO7tS5uh64Zqd503uunM0KjOzMraYQ3LDHoOKvS2ta8K3bOm9BPVdX1tlPV66fvXsNihDb1oFazIudR_MybeIcQ7f6DUSz6q6Hbmk0TzS5hRmPG6rnrLs0_fmLbiGeipCTNi-gAxpk2YsYCPEog9gzAj7yBnIJY-rfLKhVju-U2YNnn48g1MMuQiCzAWAJKY5CMWZqyKW88LY0SyD3M7PMb_LP7Rlx9n_gUAAP__LJOKmg) who will retire this year. The top-five cities also included Mansfield, Ohio; Kokomo, Indiana; Bay City, Michigan; and Midland, Michigan. California has the worst housing market for retirees, led by San Jose. Eleven of the bottom 20 markets are found in the Golden State.

**Whatever happened to these classic American dinners?** There was a time when the American dinner table ran on pantry confidence: a few cans, a box, and a pound of meat to produce heartfelt meals that weren't meant to impress food critics. They were trying to feed families, stretch paychecks, and turn Tuesday night into something warm. Take [tuna noodle casserole](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOszAQBOCngQ5kb4INhYu_ocvfXokcewl7MjZiN0G8_Yl7hZt69I00X4_p4SlRfv33KzqZy5Tx4IQiuE8UcNuLIAu_OeKWysnryYITKDCN0o3WU_SUzuleRzcjAt5qdNqaXsPQdUO9OKtMr3WIT-z1M6i-6yP4OBiw3kDUtiZ3aUpfUV1nW1DG62BtPwQz3Gdb3RVGkrK334WyLDins3xwb0NZ6-QWkY2r278KxgrG4zha3jDHg2TZMGdCvooVjOj5bOSdfRM8M-4lYQVjvbu4HZQSsbmmXqun9EvLn_74OPgJAAD__3zYdaw), the beige, beloved workhorse of mid-century kitchens. With canned tuna, egg noodles, and cream-of-something soup, it was comfort food by formula--cheap, filling, and reliably familiar. It faded when processed foods became suspect and when weeknight cooking shifted from "make it last" to "make it fresh," even if fresh meant pricier and more time-consuming. [Shake 'n Bake chicken ](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO6yAURL8GulhwHYMpKN7Tbrpst0qJMFzHRDhY5iaW_37l_YWdckY6I53b1V19yul5__IzWhqLe-JWMxLh6lLAZS2EleqrRlxy2eu8V0IHAtRJyJOULvqUd3fm0Y6IgC1HK7XqJZiuM3yysQejB40x6igwgInjYILC0IOGLhie7EET8ojoOt2AUF4GrXsTlDmPmp0FxkRlbR4lPWnCMe_ljWsTysyznYiWytp_DC4MLtu2NXt50WvAYz8aT2Fi7eXN2o_hv3609ftWpk--2rhsKedU1XFxn33Kv0j6k4e3hZ8AAAD___nQciU)offered a different kind of magic: the thrill of crispiness without the mess. You shook chicken pieces in a bag of seasoned crumbs and baked your way to crunchy satisfaction. But the box began to look like training wheels as air fryers, scratch breading, and ingredient-label vigilance reframed convenience as compromise. Salisbury steak--ground beef shaped into patties and smothered in gravy--was diner dignity on a budget, a way to make humble meat feel like a proper plate. Over time, it became inseparable [from TV-dinner trays](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAUheGnsbdYcIkhHhiqSm6lKh06tCMicIlpcbDgJpbfvnJfoWc90jf8X2dztjHF2_XdzqgpZHPDtSYkwmKiw6Vkwkr1Xj0uKW913iqhAQbywPiBc-NtTJs5tl4HREDRouZKnjgMfT-0k5aOgVTeeVSCnSwLVko3oA8XIZj0qo161xjfx_pedcCk5U6p0-DkcAyqOTL0kXLpvnO80YQhbfmBpXN5bpOeiJbaiKcGxgbGdV27Ld_pfsH9b2CsUy5UGxjfngVcPj9e-OtPW7Rf1phSrHL3r7ON6c-jf0V4aPgNAAD__2QmcMk) and the cultural retreat from brown gravies and heavy starches. Then there's [liver and onions](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2O7CAQBODT2BkWtP8Dgpc4m5duaLGmGXqEwYIej3z7lfcKW3Gp9Km-HuvDUKD4_G921OzSGvFTAjJjXmnDIyfGwuVdLB4hXWW_CuMKEgYhlVBqtYbCtXa11Q4RsK1Rq3GYFMx9P9deO5hRzoPpZgeqw62dJmi7vrdGbqObhpr0vSbVHdn3YwNyMGobx2nehrlzY9VJtMQpN69EkT26cKUTc7OlvQ7aMx-lav9VsFSwvN6BTCm0H-Eq6c0ec7yLFSwgoatgUbKChTFazCIFK5wpnlJEK74RnQh0YhYmWpEipVjEh9iLZzbnJTJudGDjeQ911vb4UAhUhlv43A2FXxH_6cZTw08AAAD__ziGiNU), once a staple of thrift and nutrition, now mostly a dare. As supermarkets made muscle cuts more affordable and Americans grew squeamish about organ meat, its iron-rich practicality lost to preference. And ham steak with pineapple--the sweet-salty centerpiece of postwar optimism--rode the canned-fruit era's glamour. When sugar turned villain and "retro" stopped being the default, the pineapple ring slipped off the plate. None of these dinners truly disappeared; they just lost their mainstream prominence. Yet they linger in church cookbooks, family stories, and the occasional diner special--reminders that the past didn't always taste "artisanal," but it did taste familiar. _Did you enjoy any of these dinners from yesteryear? Or have a favorite recipe from this era? Let us know by replying to this email._

It helped restore bladder control, eliminate leaks, and regain confidence-- | Whatever happened to these classic American dinners? There was a time when the American dinner table ran on pantry confidence: a few cans, a box, and a pound of meat to produce heartfelt meals that weren't meant to impress food critics. They were trying to feed families, stretch paychecks, and turn Tuesday night into something warm. Take , the beige, beloved workhorse of mid-century kitchens. With canned tuna, egg noodles, and cream-of-something soup, it was comfort food by formula--cheap, filling, and reliably familiar. It faded when processed foods became suspect and when weeknight cooking shifted from "make it last" to "make it fresh," even if fresh meant pricier and more time-consuming. a different kind of magic: the thrill of crispiness without the mess. You shook chicken pieces in a bag of seasoned crumbs and baked your way to crunchy satisfaction. But the box began to look like training wheels as air fryers, scratch breading, and ingredient-label vigilance reframed convenience as compromise. Salisbury steak--ground beef shaped into patties and smothered in gravy--was diner dignity on a budget, a way to make humble meat feel like a proper plate. Over time, it became inseparable and the cultural retreat from brown gravies and heavy starches. Then there's , once a staple of thrift and nutrition, now mostly a dare. As supermarkets made muscle cuts more affordable and Americans grew squeamish about organ meat, its iron-rich practicality lost to preference. And ham steak with pineapple--the sweet-salty centerpiece of postwar optimism--rode the canned-fruit era's glamour. When sugar turned villain and "retro" stopped being the default, the pineapple ring slipped off the plate. None of these dinners truly disappeared; they just lost their mainstream prominence. Yet they linger in church cookbooks, family stories, and the occasional diner special--reminders that the past didn't always taste "artisanal," but it did taste familiar. Did you enjoy any of these dinners from yesteryear? Or have a favorite recipe from this era? | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: It started with a tweet... and now it’s war. Elon Musk just humiliated Donald Trump in front of millions — live on X . | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | It started with a tweet... and now it’s war. Their followers are tearing into each other. And it’s all happening out in the open. Insiders say this digital battle isn’t just ego — Elon’s building something bigger. A movement. A challenge to Trump’s grip on the conservative base. With over 170 million watching, this fight could reshape the future of the GOP — and America. # Sunday, January 11, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1923, legendary auto racer and designer [Carroll Shelby](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykjz2OwyAQRk8DXaIZ8B8FxTbusu2WCHvGNitsIkBJfPtVcoWtP31P7_3c3M2HGI712-9s62tJ7uBniVwrZ1f55ctvmsruoz_9UcPK2R_knQLVXQAviI58iKdrJNkJyKBki303oFZDq-RmZ-17ogY1t0YxmL5l6Bai3vDC2hgZ7BsGiIgKoMWrxnnuWiAcaEIEEA0whZrytW78UVrimR6cr3PaZbRbrfci9JdQo1Bj2ThO53sSatxCqSmfMlu6P0OMoXS9aGDdfYifd_1f88OqvwAAAP__PDhrMQ) was born in Leesburg. Shelby won the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 1959, and he partnered with British racer Ken Miles to develop the Ford GT40, which won the prestigious race from 1966 to 1969. Shelby’s name adorns many famous muscle cars, including the AC Cobra and the Mustang Shelby GT500. San Antonio’s [Cowboy Breakfast returns](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL3y2yAQxJ8GdWjuTh-WC4o0dP-0KTWHQDY2Ag8Qy3r7jPwKabbZmd3f7p-f-Yd98PH2mzen6mdNc3R7Ca5Wl-fqPlweyZSNAx8cq7-5zNHyTECjBJSIs2UfjrlvrDJgr9g4hZdxwo6mgZq7snC1NE6mX2jorO3YIg_rcgFjJ9MN1Hh1hgEiIgEM2Ha4LOMAFidrEAFED876mnJb7-6LtIYjvV1ul7Q1Qd1rfRXR_RKkBel939tn4XqagvS5RpAOaeEgSJ9VgjTgKVdBunCUHGuKPhW5ZufkknaTDmmy4-fKpcrs6t8ci3xwlB0I0k1W9rX7EHwZL6KH28Y-fGHq_134VvQvAAD__-WThHE) Jan. 30. If you’ve never experienced thousands of Texans lining up at dawn for free tacos and tequila, you’re missing one of the state’s great traditions. See you there! If you or someone you know struggles with prostate or urinary issues, today’s sponsor, [Prostadine,](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2OgzAQRk9juqAZ85NQuIgUUayUbbdEgz3ArAxGMErC7VfJFbb_9L2n93Pv7iRRlvGbZnb6GlK38HOPrMpbp_yi_Tf1-0yRDlpURt5oCdRZsPUJ8ITYBZJ4dGUWXA-hwYwdnusLFvZS2Wxy0PumgYp68mDtgA14HDz2lqjwvmwyce8zQES0ABXmBXpfVxDwEnpEAFMCB9G05TrxR2mIR3rwlvs0Z9FNqutuiquxrbHtyLpuaVcKsvB7YWz7kMApX6fVFC0Ng0QhZVPc-hQDrfxCY2uVYIrbEI8nc-CA-EWLra_Z5sL6lBhlr8-mhHEmiR-w_i_Xw9m_AAAA__8idn9q) is revealing an unusual discovery that’s getting a lot of attention.

## Texas Jobs Market Outpaces National Growth
Texas added [7,300 nonfarm jobs](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u6yAUhFdjd1gc8E9cULzGXV57S-sAB4cIQwQkTnZ_FW_htqNPo2_m57pe0Qcft_-4k6pvl9ZIRwlUK-W10hvLPemyY8APxuo3yhgtroKLkXFgAKtFHz5r31qluZ2hJQXTeAEpLoNob0qa2UoHs0AwUk6zJDdZ7LWW1M_SQevVt4wDAAjOB-gkGDMO3MLFagDOm56T9TXlrt7oVHLhk16UO5P2NqhbrY_SyH-NWBqxHMfR1cN0J9ht6dWI5TupEcsZsYA6ZeZSNsTKMz-wFCoMhpntPgSfIospOsw7uyfNTHrGynw0mbBQabOyj-PLlXFqer7t6MPpUf_23kuJ3wAAAP__WtOGgw) in November and recorded an unemployment rate of 4.2%, outperforming national trends as the state's labor force reached a record 15.9 million workers. The Texas Workforce Commission reported the state gained 146,300 jobs year-over-year, more than any other state. While national job growth was the weakest in five years, with just 50,000 positions added in December, Texas maintained modest momentum. The state's 1.0% annual job growth rate topped the [national rate of 0.6%](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkEHOmzAQhU9jdhN5TAjpgkWqCnXzd9NK7Q6N8RDcGhvZkxBuX5EeoauR3khP7_t-fgwf5IOP92-0cCevKQ2RtxJYhPMg_KLyO9myUKCdovg7Z4qOBqPNBTQC4uDIh304V66z2n3CijtsL1eszbUx1dyR43rSY9M21tjR1FeypplGW1PTTti2le-OMo2IaLRu8FTjOF4a7fDqLKLW6qzZeUn5JDO_J01hT0_OpzEtVehmkbWo-qZMr0y_bdtpKfH4KdNzhEdRpl9S5P24lP-wHMm7CA44-BfCkoqEHaZAAj5CTE9eLGewD4EiPgSw2d9n4QwyU4RI4lOkAKsf5ZFZmZ4y3G7449f6tVV1n0bvVP3l86GXt_Kd81rlzq2bD8GXS6vO-r6QD28O-T_7z878DQAA__-3EZzM), though it fell below Texas' historical 2% growth trend. Large gains came from education, health services, and professional services, while trade, transportation, and mining sectors lost positions. Amarillo and Midland recorded the state's [lowest unemployment rates](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2ynDAMhZ_GdGYs8XsLF2noNm1KRthicWLsHdtZljx9Bl7hNhoVmvN9Or8e84Ocd-H5k3bW5bPGOfCRPZfCaS78ofw7LnknTyeF4p6cKFiaUWEvFUiA2ZLz59xWVi_KfkHFGoZ-hAbHDqtNG2sQ18EOuBKMzaLAsuk7_FJjC9xg5fQVpgAAUKkO6gaM6TtlYbQLgFKiVWxdiakuG99Kqz_jm1Nt4l55vZXyyqL5IXASOB3HURvvzB_c4t9cYriuBE7XWwInHw15gdPFFDgpuMYgcLqDZeaSZWITk81yjUl6WmK6NsMyu38sKVjJ-8vHc-dQZIhv3hdO0lIhmbd4U6qk7etw3rvcD6JVz52cv3XL90p-a_wfAAD__8r0kbY), while Eagle Pass and Brownsville-Harlingen had the highest.
## Houston Marathon Street Closures Sunday
The Chevron Houston [Marathon will take over city streets](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2OqzAQxfGngS5oZvgIKVzcJl1uuyUa8BB7ZezIng3h7VfJK2x_9NPR_-s23dgHH-__eROjrzVNUfYSRFXypPLi8p3msnHgg6P6u2SOlicCGk6AJ8TJsg_H1NXWzGAvWIvB8zBiS2NPtTPcySjD2HV0sTSu80A9Lwi2XS-CLUntzRsDREQC6LFpcVmGHiyOdkYEqDoQ6zXlRp18Lq3hSE_JzZK2Ohin-ihV-6-ia0XXfd-bxckzp-jST9EUN86sLsX3vKJrnY197D4EX4Zz1cF9Yx8-lP4twNPQbwAAAP__5oNwRQ) today, drawing over 300,000 runners and spectators. Major roadways, including Washington Avenue, Kirby Drive, Memorial Drive, Allen Parkway, and Avenida de las Américas, will be closed or partially closed. Neighborhoods such as downtown, Midtown, Memorial Park, Montrose, and River Oaks are expected to experience the most significant impact. The marathon's [road closure list](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuuqzAQhldjOtDM8IhTuLgNXW57SmTHAzgyOMJDCLs_SrZw-v_x6fu5DTcbYlin_3ZhI-8xDSsfObIIb4Pw2-ZHcnmx0Z52lTDxZldvBwLqSsAScfA2xHNoCm8c-CsWbPDSaaxJt1TMxo3aNq5pawcdUKevGvSlYR6h1uTQF8F8xgARkQBarGq837sWPGrvEAFUA-yDpK2Smb9IYzzTi7fqnpYimlnkmVX9T1GvqD-Oo3qFHGROe5a0fgufpKLexTQp6p8pi6L-TPtWTnvwXEoqH3bd7XYq6ovN-OcRYgy5u6gGpsWG-P2Svxl6GfoNAAD__6G1e2k) spans 13 pages and includes freeway ramp closures from 6 a.m. today. Former radio personality Sarah Pepper returns to emcee the event, one of her favorite traditions since her years connecting with Houston listeners. Last year, the marathon raised funds for over [60 local charities](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OrDAMhZ8mdEFx-BkoUtyGbm67JTKJGbIbEpRkhuHtV6DdJ9jGsmXL55zv4z7e0TrrH_9xJZXfcxg97clRzhTHTG9Mn2FKKzo80Gf7oIje4CiFbLkADjAatO4Y68KoSZgeClJwazuoZNfIYlG6Mh0YMwlsprrXGrRpmklIROpbQ6Kw6nwmAACkEA2UFWjdNsJAZyYAIVgtyNgcYpkXuizN7ggviqUOa-HUkvOWWPWPyYHJYd_3Ujurv-QSnikHf14xOfxM3NmZmBxOTSYHAWfpmRzmEFeKPKKxgW8UU_DobD54wogL32jbzjXlZ_SJ58AvJ2dDqybiFxG90CsGz3_FVoyYl-CZHIqozLZb52xqb6wWjxWtuxLkv3F_KfkdAAD__3nfmeU).
## San Antonio's Free Cowboy Breakfast Returns
San Antonio Stock Show & Rodeo and Spurs Give will host the [free Cowboy Breakfast](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL3y2yAQxJ8GdWjuTh-WC4o0dP-0KTWHQDY2Ag8Qy3r7jPwKabbZmd3f7p-f-Yd98PH2mzen6mdNc3R7Ca5Wl-fqPlweyZSNAx8cq7-5zNHyTECjBJSIs2UfjrlvrDJgr9g4hZdxwo6mgZq7snC1NE6mX2jorO3YIg_rcgFjJ9MN1Hh1hgEiIgEM2Ha4LOMAFidrEAFED876mnJb7-6LtIYjvV1ul7Q1Qd1rfRXR_RKkBel939tn4XqagvS5RpAOaeEgSJ9VgjTgKVdBunCUHGuKPhW5ZufkknaTDmmy4-fKpcrs6t8ci3xwlB0I0k1W9rX7EHwZL6KH28Y-fGHq_134VvQvAAD__-WThHE) on Jan. 30 outside the Frost Bank Center. The event launches Rodeo season and the 2026 Rodeo BBQ Cookoff weekend, offering free breakfast tacos, coffee from 5 to 10 a.m., and free parking in select lots. The morning features live music, rodeo merchandise, and appearances by the Spurs Hype Squad. Those 21 and over can purchase alcoholic beverages, including beer and the rodeo's exclusive [Surtierra Tequila](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGO4zAMRU9jdwxE2fHYhYtt0s22WxqURCeayFQgMZPx7RcJcoNpiYeHx__vc_mkmKKc_9LGs_6seRF-1MSqXBblH6pf2dWNEu0kGs9cSAIt1tgBDALiEiimfenbMDsTJmx5xo9hxM6OR9te5mB5st6PXZisczQO3puJppFtP0wOXRvnp8wgIlpjjnjo0PvhaAKOwSEa0_SGQ9RcDnrhV9Ka9vzN5eDz1qb5onqrTfensafGnp75fSUh0SwxP5n3tbGnlD2lxp4qCbyBCj4_XN7BFabrSlWhsN6LVPgiuVPZoTNwjf4a5Qx5XaHkwBkqU80Ca2HeSEDJv1Trygz1di8VWJSLUpSNRdsyh9sjphTr8NH05rxRTK8H9Hezf8_2fwAAAP__qEScwQ), starting at 7 a.m. Students and instructors from St. Philip's College culinary program will help with cooking, and the rodeo will donate to support the program. The event continues a longtime San Antonio tradition ahead of the rodeo, which runs from [Feb. 12 through March 1](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykULuO6yAU_Brc2eLgd0FxG3e57ZbWMRwcdjFEQEL89yvnF7abhzQzmq_bekPrrN__40Eyv01YPZXkKGeKa6Y3pu-wpQMdnuiz3Smi17gKLoaaQw2warTuXLtKy43rGSqSMA4TtGLqRXWXZjZq6GfV6vGC3Ty2nTDDOLVzPyk1V1ZeYRwAQHDeQ9OCUkPPNUx6A-CcdZy0zSE2-U6fScad4UWxUeGonLzn_Eis_cfEwsRSSmkSxqApXD4Ty4OJxaCNewxPr9PFnl5HtMn6vaYX-XyJKpQtnPUWCX8MplxFqR_FOmfTMLKO7wda96nMfzvqJcVvAAAA__9kkH6M).
