name: Deb Haaland
subject: Not your typical pantsuit
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**
body:
I hope you’ll let me show you some of the places where I’ve rocked my mocs.

| | |
---
Friend,
In a minute, I’m going to ask you to make [**your first-ever $5 contribution to our grassroots campaign for New Mexico.**](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3725?refcode=jtk130-fr-nd&t=1&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4) But first, I hope you’ll let me show you some of the places where I’ve **rocked my mocs.**
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3726?refcode=jtk130-fr-nd&t=2&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4)
_My child Somáh was my biggest cheerleader on the day I was sworn in as Secretary of the Interior._
On the day I was sworn in as Secretary of the Interior, I decided to wear my white moccasins and a handmade ribbon skirt adorned with butterflies, stars, and corn. No matter where I go, I am a proud Pueblo woman first — including at the Department of the Interior.
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3726?refcode=jtk130-fr-nd&t=3&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4)
_I even rocked my mocs at the White House Tribal Nations Summit._
When I wear my mocs, I'm reminded of how powerful our traditional clothing is to our shared heritage. On the first day of the White House Tribal Nations Summit in 2021, I wore my beaded moccasins in solidarity with our Indigenous people across the country. I am incredibly grateful that together, our Administration made extraordinary accomplishments for Indian Country — enduring progress that would make our ancestors proud.
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3726?refcode=jtk130-fr-nd&t=4&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4)
_I sported my moccasins and ribbon skirt at a shiprock parade!_
From my ancestral homelands here in New Mexico to the halls of Congress and now on the campaign trail, I rock my mocs to honor the people whose shoulders I stand on. History has shown us there’s nothing we can’t accomplish when we work together, which is why I’m counting on the support of people like you as we fight for a New Mexico that works for everyone.
**No state in America has ever elected a Native American woman as governor.[Will you chip in $5 so we can build a strong campaign capable of winning this race and making history again?](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3725?refcode=jtk130-fr-nd&t=5&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4)**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Donate $10 >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3727?refcode=jtk130-fr-nd&t=6&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4)
---
| | [Donate $35 >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3728?refcode=jtk130-fr-nd&t=7&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4)
---
| | [Donate $50 >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3729?refcode=jtk130-fr-nd&t=8&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4)
---
| | [Other Amount >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3726?refcode=jtk130-fr-nd&t=9&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4)
---
I am so grateful for your support. Thank you for being by my side,

Deb 🩵
|
* * *
| | [Donate >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1101?refcode=jtk130-fr-nd&t=10&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4)
---
| | [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1102?refcode=jtk130-fr-nd&t=11&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4)
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
[**Receive Fewer Emails**](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?&t=12&akid=6734%2E400544%2EQZKpZ4)

Email us: info@debhaaland.com
**PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**

name: James Carville
subject: This is SO damn important
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
Outside spending groups will pour an UNGODLY amount of money into this race to defeat Sherrod (I’m talking hundreds of millions of dollars) – and it’s on us to give him the resources to fight back and win.
#
Friend, it’s James Carville.
I’ve worked on a heck of a lot of campaigns. Over the past 50 years, I’ve won – and I’ve lost – tough elections across the country. So believe me when I tell you:
**Sherrod Brown’s Senate race in Ohio is SO DAMN IMPORTANT, and the only way he can win it is with YOUR support – right here, right now.**
**[Please chip in every dollar you can spare to Sherrod's campaign and State Democrats Committee. He just got back in the fight, and they need folks like you by his side ASAP.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99438?refcode=em20260111-9931&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9931%2E614464%2E5yg7VF)**
We’re at a critical moment in American history. It’s like nothing I’ve ever seen before. Politicians in D.C. are raising costs, cutting veterans' benefits, closing rural hospitals, adding $3 trillion to our national debt – the list goes on.
We’re moving further and further away from being anything close to democracy. It’s freakin’ absurd!
_We have to get our country back on track – and that starts by helping Sherrod Brown win in Ohio._
Sherrod has spent his life fighting for workers and families, and he’s got the moral courage to stand up to the powerful special interests that are making life harder and more expensive for everyday people.

name: Team Powered by People
subject: The lie they tell you about young people
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240594%2E444211%2E2sHJw0)
“Young people don’t vote.” That’s the lie some pundits told Beto when he wanted to visit college campuses during his 2018 Senate race.
But Beto showed up anyway. He held organizing and voter registration events at community colleges, trade schools, four-year universities, and high schools. He was in the quad at public universities, on the campuses of Historically Black Colleges and Universities, and at coffee shops across the street from private colleges.
And it worked. **Young voter turnout skyrocketed by more than 500% during early voting!**
Beto has taken this same organizing approach to his work with Powered by People. In 2024, our volunteers met young prospective voters all across the state.
We checked their voter registration status, got them onto the rolls, personally stayed in touch with them, answered their questions, and nudged them until they cast their ballot. While young voters nationally turned out at a rate of 42%, **the young voters in our program turned out at a rate of 79%.** It worked again.
Now, we’re continuing this on-the-ground work as we go full-steam ahead towards the pivotal midterm elections. Beto will be back at the University of Texas at Austin THIS WEEK to register students and get more young people into our proven program. **And with your help, we can organize on even more campuses.**
[**Can you chip in $3 today to help us hire more organizers, recruit more volunteers, and deploy them to campuses throughout Texas? Your donation will help us execute our college campus program at a larger scale than ever before.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260111.nesuHh&refcode=ema20260111-240594&t=3&akid=240594%2E444211%2E2sHJw0)

Together, we will meet, register, and turn out the voters who will help us win big races up and down the ballot.
Thanks for being with us,
Team Powered by People

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pbp-em-2023-footer?refcode=em20260111-240594-footer&t=23&refcode2=240594_444211_2sHJw0&akid=240594%2E444211%2E2sHJw0)

The Supreme Court isn’t even pretending to be impartial anymore.

A new study from the National Bureau of Economic Research confirms the
obvious: conservative justices are ruling over and over for billionaires
and corporations — while working Americans pay the price.

Last summer, Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson warned us that this Court was
causing “irreparable harm” to our democracy. She was right. Now the
evidence is in, and it’s worse than anyone expected.

Every ruling, every decision, shows how tightly these justices are wrapped
around Trump’s finger. Loyalty, not law, guides them. Impartiality has
been erased. Public trust is crumbling. Justice has been captured.

Enough is enough.

We’re fighting back and confronting a Supreme Court too busy pandering to
Trump and his antics to actually do their jobs. But fighting a Court this
broken requires resources, pressure, and people who are ready to take a
stand.

name: Gina Hinojosa
subject: Abbott just handed Texas voter information to the DOJ on a silver platter
body:
Texans don’t want this.
# [](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)[](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)
Peter,
**Greg Abbott just handed Texas’s entire voter registration roll that includes private identifying information like birthdates, driver’s license numbers, and Social Security numbers over to the DOJ.**
Texans did _not_ vote for that.
Abbott is handing our personal data over to the federal government on a silver platter. He’ll do whatever his billionaire donors and right-wing buddies in Washington want, even when it means putting your personal information at risk.
This is part of Trump’s nationwide push to pressure states into turning over voter records, a move voting rights advocates warn could lead to privacy violations and even eligible Texans being wrongly purged from the state's database.
Texas has elections coming up — a March primary, a May runoff, and the general election in November — and Abbott is still playing politics with our voting rights.
Texans are fed up with being sold out. We deserve leadership that protects our rights, not leadership that trades them away to score political points in Washington D.C.
**[If you’re ready to fight back against Abbott’s power grabs, will you pitch in $5 to my campaign today?](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260111_1_data.21lyuq&refcode=em20260111-397&amount=5&t=1&akid=397%2E299639%2EN8Nhp0)**
[Donate $5 »](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260111_1_data.21lyuq&amount=5&refcode=em20260111-397&t=20&akid=397%2E299639%2EN8Nhp0)
Thank you.
— Gina Hinojosa

name: Her America
subject: Kids Today vs Kids Decades Ago: A Mother and Baby Boomer's Perspective
body:
Change isn't always a positive thing. This truth becomes more evident when you are from a older generation and watch the world shift in ways that leave behind more damage than improvement.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..e1Bk9BCfjeQHBzrnMI6SeglJB4HNGnrTkWLP-L8psl0?) for more
---
[](https://substack..QKQdGcpeKDq9r4NYYr42thSG4_o9Et7tOehJcsTTNAY?)
---
# [Kids Today vs Kids Decades Ago: A Mother and Baby Boomer's Perspective](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=184067842&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg0MDY3ODQyLCJpYXQiOjE3NjgxNTQzNjcsImV4cCI6MTc3MDc0NjM2NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTMwNzI3ODAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.2G-mCSSIwZVt-aMn67zleui76kaG06gEntrb-nFSyFE)
### Change isn't always a positive thing. This truth becomes more evident when you are from a older generation and watch the world shift in ways that leave behind more damage than improvement.
| | [Julie A Miller](https://substack.com/@jmnotebook)
---
| Jan 11| | | ∙| | Guest post
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/@jmnotebook)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=184067842&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg0MDY3ODQyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2ODE1NDM2NywiZXhwIjoxNzcwNzQ2MzY3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzA3Mjc4MCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.hza6IryFYAhhMMqw3xxrtajSI_CfKcQ8SRzyjVxSen8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/politibrawl/p/kids-today-vs-kids-decades-ago-a?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

The word "boomer" becoming an insult is pretty new. The best example is **["OK boomer"](https://substack.com/redirect/d42f6911-b1cd-4c7e-bf2b-b29446d711b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **-- a phrase that got really popular on TikTok and other social media. It's like the old '90s saying "talk to the hand" -- it's a dismissive comeback that's kind of mocking, like rolling your eyes at someone. Young people use it specifically to brush off older people's perspective on today issues.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6b5fe7a7-e146-4baa-8649-31a7f8ea801d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I think every young person has, at some point, dismissed comments or advice from older people -- because they felt those perspectives were outdated or out of touch with reality. I'm certainly guilty of this. I remember rolling my eyes when my parents tried to offer guidance.
As I've gotten older, I've come to understand the real value in an older person's perspective. Their opinions draw from a before-and-current view of the world -- they've lived and have experienced what was and what is now, which gives them unique insight into whether the changes we call "progress" are actually helping or hurting our society.
Now that we've established that perspectives from Baby Boomers deserve consideration rather than dismissal, let's explore how I believe today's so-called progress is actually harming all of us -- not just kids but young adults too.
* * *
### Support bold independent journalism and help us replace the mainstream media by becoming a PolitiBrawl/Her America Member today
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..MlJmqat3sAv1-TdfHNon3P8PMh9dipOr317JmZ1-Vv8?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=184067842)
* * *
## Raising Kids Today VS Decades Ago
**A childhood without digital distraction:** When I was a kid, we had our parents' full attention, and they had ours. If we wanted to talk to someone at home, we walked over and spoke to them face to face. Today, kids and parents will actually text each other from different rooms instead of taking the time to have a conversation in person. This constant face-to-face interaction built essential communication skills and, in turn, fostered the self-confidence needed to engage with others. We weren't aware of it, but this development of self-confidence happened naturally through our everyday interactions at home, that carried over to feeling more comfortable when we communicate outside the home environment.
Today, whether we want to admit it or not, genuine face-to-face communication has diminished because of these constant digital distractions.
**Freedom to Roam:** Our social lives were far more spontaneous. We had casual group hangouts rather than scheduled play dates. I remember coming home from school, parents not home yet, grabbing a snack, and immediately diving into my homework because that's what was expected. Afterward, depending on my age I'd either watch some cartoons or when older I watched soap operas like General Hospital or Dark Shadows on TV, or head outside to play with neighborhood friends until dinner time.
Weekends were even better! We were up at dawn, finished our chores, maybe watched an hour of cartoons or Soul Train on TV, then headed out into the neighborhood for the entire day. If we got hungry, we'd grab a snack at whichever friend's house we happened to be at. Thirsty? We drank from the garden hose. It was perfectly normal for our parents not to see us again until dinnertime. Our days were filled with spontaneous fun--deciding on the fly what to do next. We'd ride bikes, build forts in the woods, go sledding in winter, and so on, or even just hang out talking face to face for hours.
When we got older, going to the mall wasn't just about shopping--it was about being together. We had no phones to check, no screens competing for our attention. Just hours of conversation, laughter, and the kind of undistracted quality time that seems rare today.
This is the hidden cost for today's generation. While we thought we were just hanging out with friends, we were actually building critical life skills--problem-solving when conflicts arose, learning to communicate face-to-face, developing real confidence, and soaking up vitamin D from being outside all day. These weren't taught; they were lived. And that's what's missing now.
**Quality time together was more genuine:** We've traded imagination for convenience. Before tablets, spending time together as a family was more common and never something we had to find time for. On the weekends, we would love to go to Blockbuster and, as a family, choose a VHS movie to rent. Another activity we enjoyed was playing board games together. I loved games like Tic-Tac-Toe, Checkers, and oh, Monopoly.
We learned to entertain ourselves during travel--looking out windows, playing word games, reading, or simply learning to be patient. I remember traveling home late one night with my family in our station wagon. I was in the back trunk area, lying down, looking up at the stars and counting them. I know this all may sound lame to generations today, but these were good times.
Now a days, screens provide instant distraction at every turn. It's practical parenting for a busy world, but it raises important questions about what children miss when they're constantly occupied: the chance to be bored, to observe, to talk, and to connect with the people around them. In today's world, as a family gathers around, you will most likely see them on their phones--and the parents are leading the way.
# **The Hard Truth**
We weren't immune to challenges as kids, but today's generation faces everything we did and more. Here are some examples of what today's generation is dealing with other than digital distraction.
**Gender Confusion Back Then:** If a girl was a tomboy--preferring sports over dolls, jeans over skirts--that's all it was. No one questioned it. When her body started changing during puberty and she felt confused or frightened, parents would have a heart-to-heart. They'd explain what was happening, reassure her that it was normal, and help her navigate those difficult feelings with understanding, love and support. Growing up with all brothers, I was certainly a tomboy and grew out of that phase naturally.
* **Gender Confusion Today:** Parents need to know what's happening in our schools regarding gender identity curriculum and policies. Many districts now have protocols that allow and encourage students to socially transition without parental knowledge or consent. If this is news to you, I urge you to look into your local school's policies--you may be surprised by what you find.

**Girls Equal Rights Then:** Back then we had our own bathrooms, locker rooms, and sports teams where we competed against other girls. Title IX established these sex-based protections as **[federal law in 1972](https://substack.com/redirect/2cbc755e-4420-41fa-ac59-ea38277a2ae2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. Males found in female facilities faced serious consequences. The standard was simple: boys competed with boys, girls with girls, men with men, women with women. This wasn't debatable--it was common sense rooted in basic fairness and safety.
* **Girls Equal Rights Today:** Today's liberal policies allow males who identify as women to access female bathrooms and locker rooms--often without any warning to the girls and women using those spaces. Girls' and women's voices around their concerns and discomfort are addressed by silencing them, calling them transphobic, or even threatening them. Even more troubling, **[biological males are now competing in women's sports](https://substack.com/redirect/f3f07367-a8e1-4f5f-ba50-53934fa2fbe6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**, displacing female athletes who've dedicated years to their training. Scholarships, records, and podium spots that should go to women are instead going to male-bodied competitors. It's difficult to understand how anyone defends this, yet these liberal policies persist.

**Peer Pressure Then:** Peer pressure wasn't new to our generation. We dealt with pressure to experiment with drugs like marijuana, pressure on how we dressed, what music we listened to, fitting into specific social circles, and sometimes doing things we knew were wrong--like shoplifting. There were also expectations around gender roles, though nothing like today's focus on gender identity. The list goes on. But here's what was different: we worked through these challenges privately. Our decisions and missteps stayed between us and our immediate circle. The world didn't get to weigh in on our mistakes or judge our decisions in real time.
* **Peer Pressure Today:** The peer pressure kids face today is heartbreaking and, in many cases, dangerous. Drug experimentation, which was risky in our time, is now potentially deadly--many street drugs are laced with fentanyl, and just one mistake can be fatal. Fashion and social acceptance, once navigated privately, now play out on social media. A classmate can snap an unflattering photo or video without consent, post it with a cruel caption, and within hours the entire school has seen it. The humiliation is public and permanent. Bullying has reached unprecedented levels of cruelty in the digital age.
Even illegal behavior like shoplifting has been amplified by social media, where it's filmed, glorified, and shared as a trend. In some places, liberal policies that don't prosecute theft under $900 have emboldened this behavior further.
What's most concerning is that today's kids are navigating all of this without the developmental foundation we had. Their childhoods were saturated with screens rather than the hands-on experiences that built our resilience. It's no wonder so many are struggling with depression, anxiety and turning to suicide. The constant connectivity, relentless comparison, and overwhelming exposure to everything--good and bad--is consuming them. Many are turning to medication just to cope with a world that feels impossible to navigate.

* * *
### Support bold independent journalism and help us replace the mainstream media by becoming a PolitiBrawl/Her America Member today
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..MlJmqat3sAv1-TdfHNon3P8PMh9dipOr317JmZ1-Vv8?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=184067842)
* * *
## Where do we go from here?
There are, of course, positive aspects to the technological progress we've made, but what is disheartening is the damage it has caused--and continues to cause--to our society. Both kids and adults today have established strong habits around using their phones, tablets, and computers on social media platforms. It's become ingrained, with a firm belief that they can't live without it.
Social media is here to stay and will only continue to grow. On one hand, I believe parents can redirect this obsession with digital distraction by establishing healthier habits at home and modeling better usage for their children. On the other hand, unless every parent agreed to make these changes, those who try will constantly face pushback. Their kids will experience peer pressure and potential bullying simply because they don't use or have access to the same digital distractions as their peers.
As parents, I remember our kids begging us for cell phones when they reached 5th grade. We stood firm and didn't give in to the pressure from them. However, when they entered the higher grades and their school was miles away, we recognized the benefit of them having a cell phone for emergencies. So we got them flip phones--simple devices they could use to call us if something urgent happened.
A few years later, when the iPhone emerged, I started noticing a shift at the bus stop. The group of kids waiting there was now more focused on their iPhones than on engaging in conversation with each other. I found this troubling, which is why we decided to wait many more years before upgrading their phones. Looking back now, I had no idea I was witnessing the beginning of a decline in kids' social skills and the development of an obsession with digital devices.
So I don't know where we go from here, but I will continue to pray for our kids today and remain hopeful.
* * *

Growing up with all brothers, I was certainly a tomboy and grew out of that phase naturally. Gender Confusion Today: Parents need to know what's happening in our schools regarding gender identity curriculum and policies. Many districts now have protocols that allow and encourage students to socially transition without parental knowledge or consent. If this is news to you, I urge you to look into your local school's policies--you may be surprised by what you find. Girls Equal Rights Then: Back then we had our own bathrooms, locker rooms, and sports teams where we competed against other girls. Title IX established these sex-based protections as . Males found in female facilities faced serious consequences. The standard was simple: boys competed with boys, girls with girls, men with men, women with women. This wasn't debatable--it was common sense rooted in basic fairness and safety. Girls Equal Rights Today: Today's liberal policies allow males who identify as women to access female bathrooms and locker rooms--often without any warning to the girls and women using those spaces. Girls' and women's voices around their concerns and discomfort are addressed by silencing them, calling them transphobic, or even threatening them. Even more troubling, , displacing female athletes who've dedicated years to their training. Scholarships, records, and podium spots that should go to women are instead going to male-bodied competitors. It's difficult to understand how anyone defends this, yet these liberal policies persist. Peer Pressure Then: Peer pressure wasn't new to our generation. We dealt with pressure to experiment with drugs like marijuana, pressure on how we dressed, what music we listened to, fitting into specific social circles, and sometimes doing things we knew were wrong--like shoplifting. There were also expectations around gender roles, though nothing like today's focus on gender identity. The list goes on. But here's what was different: we worked through these challenges privately. Our decisions and missteps stayed between us and our immediate circle. The world didn't get to weigh in on our mistakes or judge our decisions in real time. Peer Pressure Today: The peer pressure kids face today is heartbreaking and, in many cases, dangerous. Drug experimentation, which was risky in our time, is now potentially deadly--many street drugs are laced with fentanyl, and just one mistake can be fatal. Fashion and social acceptance, once navigated privately, now play out on social media. A classmate can snap an unflattering photo or video without consent, post it with a cruel caption, and within hours the entire school has seen it. The humiliation is public and permanent. Bullying has reached unprecedented levels of cruelty in the digital age. Even illegal behavior like shoplifting has been amplified by social media, where it's filmed, glorified, and shared as a trend. In some places, liberal policies that don't prosecute theft under $900 have emboldened this behavior further. What's most concerning is that today's kids are navigating all of this without the developmental foundation we had. Their childhoods were saturated with screens rather than the hands-on experiences that built our resilience. It's no wonder so many are struggling with depression, anxiety and turning to suicide. The constant connectivity, relentless comparison, and overwhelming exposure to everything--good and bad--is consuming them. Many are turning to medication just to cope with a world that feels impossible to navigate. Support bold independent journalism and help us replace the mainstream media by becoming a PolitiBrawl/Her America Member today Where do we go from here? There are, of course, positive aspects to the technological progress we've made, but what is disheartening is the damage it has caused--and continues to cause--to our society. Both kids and adults today have established strong habits around using their phones, tablets, and computers on social media platforms. It's become ingrained, with a firm belief that they can't live without it. Social media is here to stay and will only continue to grow. On one hand, I believe parents can redirect this obsession with digital distraction by establishing healthier habits at home and modeling better usage for their children. On the other hand, unless every parent agreed to make these changes, those who try will constantly face pushback. Their kids will experience peer pressure and potential bullying simply because they don't use or have access to the same digital distractions as their peers. As parents, I remember our kids begging us for cell phones when they reached 5th grade. We stood firm and didn't give in to the pressure from them. However, when they entered the higher grades and their school was miles away, we recognized the benefit of them having a cell phone for emergencies. So we got them flip phones--simple devices they could use to call us if something urgent happened. A few years later, when the iPhone emerged, I started noticing a shift at the bus stop. The group of kids waiting there was now more focused on their iPhones than on engaging in conversation with each other. I found this troubling, which is why we decided to wait many more years before upgrading their phones. Looking back now, I had no idea I was witnessing the beginning of a decline in kids' social skills and the development of an obsession with digital devices. So I don't know where we go from here, but I will continue to pray for our kids today and remain hopeful. | | POLL Are children missing the same level of human connection we grew up with? | ? ? ? * | A guest post by| well today means aging well tomorrow. The motivation behind my newsletters is to inspire you to live a healthy and active lifestyle. :) You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Politibrawl 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: A few people are collecting government checks that have nothing to do with taxes or Social Security. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | A few people are collecting government checks that have nothing to do with taxes or Social Security. | No applications. clean_body: I don’t have much time left| | | | | Friend, if we haven’t met yet, my name is Saritha. I’m the former Trump Nominee and Federal Prosecutor who’s running to defeat the darling of the radical Left: Letitia James. I won’t be holding back during this campaign. She deserves to be exposed and held accountable, but I won’t be able to if I cannot match her fundraising efforts. Help me defeat this corrupt liberal. While violent criminals cycle through the system, Letitia James has spent years chasing headlines and pursuing a politically motivated vendetta against President Trump, burning millions of taxpayer dollars in the process. That’s not justice. That’s politics. I know this race won’t be easy. But it can be won — and it must be won. Thank you for standing with me in this fight. — Saritha Komatireddy Candidate for Attorney General | | Paid for by Saritha for New York | |

name: Ximena, United We Dream
subject: Thanks to you
body:
| | | Peter, When we asked for your help, you stepped up, and we couldn’t be more grateful. Thanks to you, we closed the books on 2025 with more than $87,500 raised in our end-of-year drive. We’re going to put those funds to good use. **This year, we’ll continue to train hundreds of young leaders and bring young people together to share tactics and build coordinated national power.** Thanks to you, we can continue to use our train-the-trainer models to multiply our capacity. **Your generosity supports this vital work — funding trainings, gatherings, and organizing infrastructure that make everything else possible. It helps keep our community strong, vital, and united.** We hope you continue to support us in the new year, because the authoritarian threats to our community aren’t ending anytime soon. name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: We will not accept terror on our streets
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20260111-1808-foote&t=1001&akid=1808%2E342411%2EB24mgM ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Imagine sitting in your living room, hearing a knock at the door — and
knowing it could be ICE, coming to take your family, your neighbor, your
friend.

This isn’t just a nightmare... it’s happening right now. Across the country,
ICE is terrorizing our communities, targeting innocent people, and acting
with complete impunity.

Most recently in Minnesota, it turned deadly: a woman was shot and killed
in her car by an ICE agent. In the past year alone, more than 30 people
have died in ICE custody, and federal agents have fired on people 14
times, resulting in at least four deaths.

This tragedy was preventable. Leaders warned this would happen if ICE kept
escalating — and yet, here we are. And even in the wake of this tragedy,
Donald Trump, Kristi Noem, and other Republicans are claiming that the ICE
officer acted in “self-defense," when the video and eyewitness testimony
tell a very different story.

Families are mourning. Children are scared. Communities are traumatized.

The Squad is fighting back. We cannot let this become the new normal — and
we cannot wait for another life to be lost. But we can stop it. Together,
our collective voices can hold ICE accountable and demand an end to these
violent, fear-inducing raids.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/signup/jan-2026-abolish-ice/?source=em20260111-1808&t=1002&akid=1808%2E342411%2EB24mgM ]Sign our petition to Abolish ICE. Make it clear that we will not accept
terror on our streets, and that we will fight for every child, every
family, and every community that deserves safety and justice.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/signup/jan-2026-abolish-ice/?source=em20260111-1808&t=1003&akid=1808%2E342411%2EB24mgM ]ADD YOUR NAME

Thank you,

The Squad Victory Fund

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1808%2E342411%2EB24mgM

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

* * *
The circus is in town, but nobody’s laughing. Donald Trump has bulldozed the East Wing and turned the West Wing into a clubhouse for the most lawless, corrupt, and incompetent cast of characters in American history. We aren’t just sitting back and watching the show. It’s time for an IMPEACHMENTPALOOZA - a massive, nationwide effort to blast the truth onto billboards and elect a Democratic House that will finally hand out the pink slips these traitors deserve. **We’re taking the fight to the "worst of the worst":**
* **Kash Patel:** Ready to burn the FBI down to protect his boss while using taxpayer funded jets for his booty calls.
* **RFK Jr.:** Lied during his confirmation hearing and his anti-science and anti-vax agenda are a literal danger to our health.
* **Pete Hegseth:** Deployed troops on American streets and he is murdering people in international waters with no due process or evidence of a crime.
* **Pam Bondi:** In contempt of Congress for breaking the law and not releasing all of the Epstein Files to protect Trump.
* **Kristi Noem:** Caging innocent people and**** trading our rights and the rule of law for Trump’s approval.

And of course, the ringleader of grift himself: felon, fraud, adjudicated rapist and long time best friend of Jeffrey Epstein, **Donald Trump.** Friend, they think they’re untouchable. They think we’re exhausted. They are wrong. Donald Trump has bulldozed the East Wing and turned the West Wing into a clubhouse for the most lawless, corrupt, and incompetent cast of characters in American history. We aren’t just sitting back and watching the show. It’s time for an IMPEACHMENTPALOOZA - a massive, nationwide effort to blast the truth onto billboards and elect a Democratic House that will finally hand out the pink slips these traitors deserve. RFK Jr.: Lied during his confirmation hearing and his anti-science and anti-vax agenda are a literal danger to our health. Pete Hegseth: Deployed troops on American streets and he is murdering people in international waters with no due process or evidence of a crime. Pam Bondi: In contempt of Congress for breaking the law and not releasing all of the Epstein Files to protect Trump. Kristi Noem: Caging innocent people and trading our rights and the rule of law for Trump’s approval. And of course, the ringleader of grift himself: felon, fraud, adjudicated rapist and long time best friend of Jeffrey Epstein, Donald Trump. Friend, they think they’re untouchable. But I couldn't ignore what still keeps me up at night: COVID. Fauci. And the lies we were told. The lockdowns. The mandates. The fear. The power grabs. clean_body: I want to be honest with you, Peter. | | Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this | | I want to be honest with you, Peter. When I sat down to put together my , I could have focused on the usual Washington nonsense - overpriced contracts, bloated agencies, and waste hiding in plain sight. But I couldn't ignore what still keeps me up at night: COVID. Fauci. And the lies we were told. The lockdowns. The mandates. The fear. The power grabs. And worst of all -- the total lack of accountability. So this year, I made a decision: Here's what we uncovered: | | THESE ARE THE COVID-ERA ABUSES WASHINGTON WANTS YOU TO FORGET 🦇 EcoHealth was awarded $54 million from USAID to collect bat coronaviruses and transport them to Wuhan, China, for gain-of-function research. 💉 HHS spent more than $40 billion utilizing influencers to pressure racial and ethnic minority groups to get the COVID shot. 🐶 NIH extended funding to the tune of $13.8 million to continue Dr. Fauci's experiment on beagles. 🏫 Schools received almost $200 billion in COVID relief funds. What did they spend it on? Rooms at Caesars Palace, renting out MLB stadiums, and ice cream trucks. | | Peter, that's your money, and not a single bureaucrat has been held accountable! Dr. Fauci walked away. Washington moved on. And the same people who caused this mess are still in charge. I REFUSE to accept that. I'm fighting to expose the truth, hold Fauci accountable for his role in the pandemic, and put a stop to the reckless spending that made all of this possible. If you're as angry as I am... If you believe the American people deserve answers... If you're tired of Washington wasting trillions with zero consequences... | | | | | | | Your donation helps me continue digging, demanding answers, and standing up to the bureaucrats who think they're above the law. They want this buried. I won 't let that happen. In liberty, Rand Paul | | | | Paid for by Rand Paul for Senate | | P.O. clean_body: This won’t take long, Derek | | | = Well, Derek, we made it. The election year. I've been doing this job long enough to know what that means. The pace picks up. The stakes get higher. The truth of the matter is, with Donald Trump in the White House and Republicans in total control of Congress, the Senate is our last check on this corrupt administration. Every Senate seat Democrats win is another vote demanding answers, another voice calling out the chaos, another roadblock to their worst ideas. But Republicans know that, too. That’s why my race could be one of Republicans' top targets, Derek. They know there's no path to a Democratic majority without this seat. They've been planning for this. They're not going to wait around. Neither are we. Our team is already on the ground mobilizing supporters and collecting petition signatures to secure my place on the ballot. But we have a lot of work to do in the coming months to make sure we can compete with the massive fundraising hauls that Republicans are racking up. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately: = = = = = = Going forward, this campaign is going to need every bit of support it can get. I'm grateful to have you with me. The fight continues, Mark Before you go: Republicans will spend whatever it takes to hold onto power, no matter how unpopular their agenda is with voters. And they know that there’s no Senate majority for Democrats without Virginia. =****** | | Friends of Mark Warner 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States | Click here to . | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner

House Freedom Caucus member and former Navy SEAL Rep. Eli Crane has a clear message about the Freedom Caucus Foundation's mission: ** **

_"The Freedom Caucus reminds me of my time in Special Forces—an elite but outnumbered team fighting the swamp, driving the agenda, and safeguarding our American freedom._

_We started, led, and finished the fight against Biden's Green New Scam, worked with President Trump, removed illegal immigrants from Medicaid and welfare programs, and cut $1.5 trillion in spending._

_None of this would have happened without the Freedom Caucus."_

name: Team Jena
subject: Why caucusing matters
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-jg?refcode=260111-caucusemail [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-jg?refcode=260111-caucusemail]
Peter –
Caucus is one of the most important and most overlooked parts of our democracy.
It’s where grassroots support turns into real momentum. It’s how we show, together, that people power still matters. And it's the way that Jena will earn a spot on the Colorado ballot!
Jena recorded this short video about why caucusing is so critical, and why showing up truly makes a difference. Watch it here. [https://www.facebook.com/reel/1402146164947719]
https://www.facebook.com/reel/1402146164947719 [https://www.facebook.com/reel/1402146164947719]
As Secretary of State, Jena has spent her career protecting the right to vote and strengthening our democratic process. Now, as a candidate for Attorney General, she’s asking supporters like you to take the next step and be part of that process directly.
Will you commit to caucus for Jena? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/rLuIVUXy80-xxMPfbrB89g2]
By committing now, you’re helping us build a strong, organized grassroots team that will ensure Jena gets a place on the ballot. This is a crucial step in this campaign and election!
Caucus is about neighbors talking to neighbors, communities standing together, and people refusing to sit on the sidelines. Jena is in this fight because she believes in that kind of democracy — and she hopes she can count on you to stand with her.
Commit to caucus for Jena today! [https://secure.ngpvan.com/rLuIVUXy80-xxMPfbrB89g2]
Thank you for being part of Team Jena.
Onward,
Team Griswold
P.S. Caucus participation can make the difference between winning and losing. Please take a moment to watch the video and commit today – it truly matters. [https://www.facebook.com/reel/1402146164947719]
Follow Jena Online:

WOW, team – we’re holding back tears!!!

Despite Trump’s authoritarian pressure campaign, Justice Sonia Sotomayor is holding TRUTH to POWER.

She’s actively calling out Trump’s vile, racist, unconstitutional and blatantly evil abuses of power.

**We need to let her know how proud we are, but we can’t find any record of support in your city?!**
[ **Please, we still need 4,902 signatures! We’re COUNTING ON YOU to represent local Democrats immediately and sign our petition standing with Justice Sonia Sotomayor before midnight →**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71887?t=8&akid=23813%2E4449941%2EFZo9eI)

**We’ll be frank, team:** we’re deeply scared about the direction our country is heading.

But we feel safe knowing that democratic heroes like Sonia Sotomayor are doing everything in their power to fight back against Trump and his MAGA cronies.
[](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71887?t=10&akid=23813%2E4449941%2EFZo9eI)
We want to show Justice Sotomayor that Americans across the country have her back in her fight against Trump, but last time we checked our data, **we couldn’t find any record of your support, team?!**

_This just can’t be right!_

That’s why we’re absolutely counting on your signature today. [We’re sending this petition to Washington FIRST THING tomorrow morning, so we’re racing to gather 10,000 signatures from local Democrats before midnight.](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71887?t=11&akid=23813%2E4449941%2EFZo9eI)

name: Not again
subject: Rerun from hell for Wisconsin Dems
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DEFEND OUR COURTS, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE
body:
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001iKEdbmu60vPnfLeXBOUMJbSx0T0UZG3DbZNVetlCnca7-IOVdNG_9-qXp-MFQdmpOko6cXvZQzj2a5E23jrCYGdpePP8j16hDQsyCp6HcHRZRxTDDPPAs8pdP0G_2klQwLEnPFhBS-gKJSc23nJo1MFU0Ye6aSaE6hWuQg2fRNqHG55gzcSSqX9ggYIPUbayTeoeWfATAuJaPgzDmucm_m2Aw2ru_6FCQYv0EkhQNt4=&c=dgSOleXxh_HL0zqgjfI9SAfpAYResFCBW0XMgt3-991AkJU-T920Sw==&ch=hwXIW8XUk01oUrGBktzYMvb9D0qxoFRE1OPcx5YRhhKCBh08G0EL9Q==)
---
| Peter,
The first major test of this year for Democrats is not a congressional or senate race.
In April, the Wisconsin Supreme Court is back on the ballot, and MAGA has their eyes fixated on the court again.
You remember what happened last spring. Elon Musk dumped over $20 million into the race to try and buy the court. Democrats fought back, raising enough money from small dollar donations and outorganized the richest man in the world.
It embarrassed Trump so badly he threw Musk out of the White House and his role at DOGE. Then Elon and Trump had a public breakup, and Musk mostly vanished from politics for the rest of 2025.
But now, Elon is back... just in time for Wisconsin Dems to kick his ass again.
---
| [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001iKEdbmu60vPnfLeXBOUMJbSx0T0UZG3DbZNVetlCnca7-IOVdNG_9-qXp-MFQdmpOko6cXvZQzj2a5E23jrCYGdpePP8j16hDQsyCp6HcHRZRxTDDPPAs8pdP0G_2klQwLEnPFhBS-gKJSc23nJo1MFU0Ye6aSaE6hWuQg2fRNqHG55gzcSSqX9ggYIPUbayTeoeWfATAuJaPgzDmucm_ortA1Y8XXH4YFrRdYWrWNI=&c=dgSOleXxh_HL0zqgjfI9SAfpAYResFCBW0XMgt3-991AkJU-T920Sw==&ch=hwXIW8XUk01oUrGBktzYMvb9D0qxoFRE1OPcx5YRhhKCBh08G0EL9Q==)
---
Trump and Elon truly are made for each other, wholly incapable of taking "no" for an answer.
---
It's the rerun from hell.
We have four months to organize another defense of this court and MAGA sees it as their opening to claw their way back while Democrats are focused on the House and Senate. Elon is out from the shadows and bankrolling MAGA again and they're plotting revenge for what happened in 2025.
Just like last year, this race will set the tone for Democrats in 2026.
Defend Our Courts is suiting up for this battle. After our huge success in helping protect the PA Supreme Court in November, we are expanding nationwide. Wisconsin is our first major target and we are bringing the same winning formula forward.
But here is the reality. When we launched our defense of PA, we had six months to build our operation. For Wisconsin, we only have four. Two fewer months to raise the money, build the program, and get ready to defeat MAGA again.
We don't have time to waste, we have to ramp up our efforts NOW.

name: Team Jayapal
subject: A hopeful vision for a better future
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
The news over the past few months has been a lot to take in.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20260111-5802-footer&t=1&akid=5802%2E420207%2E23Ckg9)
Peter,
The news over the past few months has been a lot to take in: ICE is conducting violent raids, detaining anyone they want, and recklessly using lethal force. Health care costs are soaring for working people who are already struggling to pay the bills. And Donald Trump seems determined to get our nation entangled in more endless wars.
We know there’s a lot of fear out there — but we can’t let that fear stop us from taking action. That’s how authoritarians win.
**And we also can’t let fear be the only thing that motivates us. Pramila has said before that Democrats can’t just be an opposition party. We also have to be a proposition party that advances a hopeful vision for a better future.**
That’s why, in addition to speaking out against Trump’s authoritarian agenda, Pramila is continuing to focus on building support for progressive policies that will make a real, positive difference for working people. Policies like:
* **Medicare For All** , the only universal health care plan in Congress that will make sure _everyone_ can afford care. A recent poll even shows it’s popular in battleground House districts!

* **Just immigration reform** , so we can fix our broken system and stop politicians like Donald Trump from playing politics with immigrants’ lives.

* **Raising the minimum wage** so that _no one_ who works full time struggles to afford to live.

But Peter, building the movement we’ll need to defeat MAGA at the polls and elect a government that will actually enact this progressive vision is going to take a lot of work — and since Pramila doesn’t take a dime from corporate PACs or lobbyists, she’s counting on your grassroots support to keep up the fight.
[**If you’re with us in the fight for a better future, will you pitch in $3 today to support the work Pramila’s doing to build our movement?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20260111.K0IdsI&refcode=em20260111-5802&amount=3&t=2&akid=5802%2E420207%2E23Ckg9)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20260111.K0IdsI&amount=3&refcode=em20260111-5802&t=21&akid=5802%2E420207%2E23Ckg9)**
In solidarity,
Team Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20260111-5802-footer&t=22&refcode2=5802_420207_23Ckg9&akid=5802%2E420207%2E23Ckg9)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=23&akid=5802%2E420207%2E23Ckg9)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=5802%2E420207%2E23Ckg9)

subject: You may have already heard
body:
The far-right is prepared to spend millions

| |

name: Chris Taylor HQ
subject: Not a scare tactic
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Taylor for Justice
body:
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20260111-1003&t=1&akid=1008%2E294397%2EnCFbl1)
Right-wing special interests are getting ready to pour _tens of millions of dollars_ into Wisconsin to try to buy a Supreme Court seat.
That’s not a scare tactic — it’s exactly what they’ve done before.
But here’s what they can’t buy: A people-powered campaign made up of thousands of grassroots supporters stepping up together.
You see, when one person gives $3, another does too. Then another.
And together, we create a surge of momentum that beats dark money every time.
With your support, we’ll elect Judge Chris Taylor to the Wisconsin Supreme Court and protect our fundamental freedoms.
[**Chip in $3 or whatever you can right now to help us take on right-wing special interests — and _win:_**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/204?c0=em_ms_jct_20260111_1_ak.TQZzDC&refcode=em20260111-1003&t=3&akid=1008%2E294397%2EnCFbl1)

Thanks so much,
Team Taylor

name: TN House Democratic Caucus
subject: We need to tell you about January 13th
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_
body:
We hope you’re getting back into the swing of things now that the holiday break has passed. We are busy preparing for the tough legislative session ahead.
| | | | Can't see this email? [View in Your Browser](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KEQ6BV2XEBGD9E3M7JDWGJ4F_0)
---
| | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KEQ6BV2XEBGD9E3M7JDWGJ4F_1)
---
| | | | Dear Peter, We hope you’re getting back into the swing of things now that the holiday break has passed. We are busy preparing for the tough legislative session ahead. We're writing today because we want to be direct with you about something very important.

Soon, the Tennessee General Assembly convenes. The moment our session begins, campaign finance rules prohibit us from accepting financial contributions. We won't be able to fundraise again until the legislature convenes.

**[While this will spare you from more of these emails for a while, it means that we have less than a week to raise the funds we need to communicate, organize and mobilize throughout the legislative session. We are so grateful for your support - anything you can give now will make a meaningful difference.](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KEQ6BV2XEBGD9E3M7JDWGJ4F_2)**
---
| | [ SUPPORT THE CAUCUS BEFORE THE DEADLINE ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KEQ6BV2XEBGD9E3M7JDWGJ4F_3)
---
| We don't take your support for granted. We know there are many worthy causes asking for your attention. But if the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus is something you believe in, we're asking you to donate now.

**Every contribution made before January 13th matters.**
---
| | [ SUPPORT THE CAUCUS BEFORE THE DEADLINE ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KEQ6BV2XEBGD9E3M7JDWGJ4F_4)
---
| Thank you for considering our request. We're deeply grateful for supporters like you who make our work possible.

With appreciation, **Tennessee House Democratic Caucus**
---
| | | |
---
| _Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_

Team, more people need to be talking about this: **_Hegseth announces censure and potential demotions of Sen. Mark Kelly
\- The Washington Post_** **Pete Hegseth and the Trump administration are weaponizing the government to target Mark Kelly, going after his pension and insulting his brave military service.** Why? Because Mark Kelly _dared_ to speak out. name: Team Wes Moore
subject: 1 day left…
body:
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=1186%2E1086043%2EuqfC6Y)
Before we shut down our fundraising for the next three months on Tuesday night, we have to ask: **[Can you pitch in $5 or more to help our campaign before we stop fundraising on 1/14?](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260111-1.U_7_ma&refcode=em-20260111-1&t=2&akid=1186%2E1086043%2EuqfC6Y)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260111-1.U_7_ma&amount=5&refcode=em-20260111-1&t=3&akid=1186%2E1086043%2EuqfC6Y)**
Peter, we don’t take a single donation for granted. We know that when you give, you’re trusting Wes to get the work done — and **that’s exactly what he’ll do heading into Maryland’s 449th legislative session.**
Since Wes became governor, the mission has been simple: Work, Wages, and Wealth. We’re making record investments in Maryland to make sure that vision is a reality for all of us.
Wes is fighting to put Maryland first, but we have a major obstacle to our reelection campaign. We’re about to stop fundraising for nearly three full months. **That means we need to amplify our fundraising efforts right now.**
_That’s where you come in, Peter._
**[Making an $5 contribution right now can be a simple, significant way to make a difference for our campaign during the fundraising freeze. So please, Peter, contribute $5 or more right now so we can hit our $62,000 goal.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260111-1.U_7_ma&refcode=em-20260111-1&t=4&akid=1186%2E1086043%2EuqfC6Y)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260111-1.U_7_ma&amount=5&refcode=em-20260111-1&t=23&akid=1186%2E1086043%2EuqfC6Y)**
Thank you for all that you do,
Team Moore

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20260111-1&t=24&refcode2=1186_1086043_uqfC6Y&akid=1186%2E1086043%2EuqfC6Y)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=25&akid=1186%2E1086043%2EuqfC6Y)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=26&akid=1186%2E1086043%2EuqfC6Y)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

name: Schiff HQ
subject: NOAA and USDA Budget Cuts
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
| Adam is working to provide CA families with all the resources they need.
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=9399%2E5257348%2ECJHeOj)
Derek —
In recent years, California has faced a torrent of dangerous weather, including fires, earthquakes, and storms that have ravaged our local communities.
These disasters are moments where our friends, neighbors, first responders, and governments step up for one another at a time of need.
_Except for the Trump admin._ They are taking a different approach.
_Instead of expanding support for families, they are cutting funding and staff from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) and the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA)._
Adam and his fellow CA Senator, Alex Padilla, are fighting back and are calling on the Trump administration to reverse these cuts.
[](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q12026_abs_em.43HrbP&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260111_AM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=9399%2E5257348%2ECJHeOj)
These cuts threaten California's ability to prepare for dangerous storms and floods, posing risks to public safety, property, and agriculture.
Adam is ready to keep fighting the Trump admin to ensure Californians have access to the resources and support they need.
**[Derek, if you’re able to pitch in today to support Adam’s work, it would mean a lot. Please, pitch in $10 or more to help Adam keep up the fight for Californians.](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q12026_abs_em.43HrbP&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260111_AM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=9399%2E5257348%2ECJHeOj)******

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: frustrated...
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HICKENLOOPER FOR COLORADO
body:
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123139017/594479132/-1473932214?refcode=logo&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDdhOGNlYy0yM2VmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JX-51YJC2X02Q3F-Arf_00qYgHs3ZBtwsV1kHWwqyrw=) We’ve been hearing from folks all across Colorado – at town halls, at community events, and as we go about our days – and one thing is abundantly clear: **People are just as frustrated as I am, and I bet you are too.** Donald Trump is doing everything he can to punish Colorado for not voting for him and falling in line with his agenda. He’s trying to dismantle NCAR, a critical climate research facility in Boulder. He rejected disaster relief funding requests. He vetoed a bill that would’ve provided clean drinking water to rural Coloradans. And he moved Space Command out of Colorado Springs. Frankly, I’m sick of it. **So my promise to you is this: I will never stop fighting back during this unprecedented and critical moment.** We’re challenging Trump and extreme Republicans’ unconstitutional plans in court. We’re opposing irresponsible appointees, like Trump’s disastrous nomination of Steve Pearce who would love to sell off public land. We’re building a national movement that will reject this administration’s never-ending cycle of chaos and destruction. And we’re focusing on electing leaders who will move our country forward, not backwards. But I’m sure Donald Trump would love nothing more than to flip our Senate seat and send another MAGA Republican to the Senate. We need to be united and build a massive grassroots effort to hold Donald Trump accountable and put an end to Republicans’ total control of Washington. So today, I’m coming to you: **_[Can you make a donation now to help us keep fighting to protect Colorado from Trump’s dangerous agenda?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123139018/594479133/-1838607967?amounts=5,15,25,50,100&amount=15&refcode=email_011126_frustratedrf&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDdhOGNlYy0yM2VmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JX-51YJC2X02Q3F-Arf_00qYgHs3ZBtwsV1kHWwqyrw=)_** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123139019/594479134/-1838607967?express_lane=true&amounts=5,15,25,50,100&amount=5&refcode=email_011126_frustratedrf&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDdhOGNlYy0yM2VmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JX-51YJC2X02Q3F-Arf_00qYgHs3ZBtwsV1kHWwqyrw=)

clean_body: = We’ve been hearing from folks all across Colorado – at town halls, at community events, and as we go about our days – and one thing is abundantly clear: People are just as frustrated as I am, and I bet you are too. Donald Trump is doing everything he can to punish Colorado for not voting for him and falling in line with his agenda. He’s trying to dismantle NCAR, a critical climate research facility in Boulder. He rejected disaster relief funding requests. He vetoed a bill that would’ve provided clean drinking water to rural Coloradans. And he moved Space Command out of Colorado Springs. Frankly, I’m sick of it. So my promise to you is this: I will never stop fighting back during this unprecedented and critical moment. We’re challenging Trump and extreme Republicans’ unconstitutional plans in court. We’re opposing irresponsible appointees, like Trump’s disastrous nomination of Steve Pearce who would love to sell off public land. We’re building a national movement that will reject this administration’s never-ending cycle of chaos and destruction. And we’re focusing on electing leaders who will move our country forward, not backwards. But I’m sure Donald Trump would love nothing more than to flip our Senate seat and send another MAGA Republican to the Senate. We need to be united and build a massive grassroots effort to hold Donald Trump accountable and put an end to Republicans’ total control of Washington. body:
[ https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/685?t=1001&akid=3605%2E898137%2EWWxbjY ][IMG]

We're excited,
team! The Hill named our primary one of the most exciting
to watch in 2026!

[ https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/4?c0=mam-em-jan2026.Z4k7f1&refcode=AU_MAM_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20260111_PM1_V1_S1&t=1002&akid=3605%2E898137%2EWWxbjY ]The Hill: The most exciting primaries to watch in 2026

Lists like this are always nice to see, but what really counts is our
people power and our reach — and you can help us keep building in these
crucial early months of 2026.

We’re going to be busy from now until April, making sure we’ve got more
than enough signatures to be on that primary ballot. We’re taking nothing
for granted — so we want to turn in the maximum number of signatures
allowed to account for any duplicates, errors, or disqualified signatures.

[ https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/702?c0=mam-em-jan2026.Nk60VF&refcode=AU_MAM_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20260111_PM1_V1_S1&t=1003&akid=3605%2E898137%2EWWxbjY ]That will help us set up for a winning campaign all the way to
November. Can you pitch in $20.26 to help carry us through?

Let’s show them what exciting really is!

— Team Mallory
